pli-tv-kd14:13.3.17.eng: While we were arguing and disputing, we did and said many things unworthy of monastics.

pli-tv-kd14:13.3.18.pli: Sace mayaṁ imāhi āpattīhi aññamaññaṁ kāressāma, siyāpi taṁ adhikaraṇaṁ kakkhaḷattāya vāḷattāya bhedāya saṁvatteyya.
pli-tv-kd14:13.3.18.eng: If we deal with one another for these offenses, this legal issue might lead to harshness, nastiness, and schism.

pli-tv-kd14:13.3.19.pli: Yadi saṅghassa pattakallaṁ, ahaṁ yā ceva imesaṁ āyasmantānaṁ āpatti, yā ca attano āpatti, imesañceva āyasmantānaṁ atthāya, attano ca atthāya, saṅghamajjhe tiṇavatthārakena deseyyaṁ, ṭhapetvā thullavajjaṁ, ṭhapetvā gihippaṭisaṁyuttaṁ.
pli-tv-kd14:13.3.19.eng: If the Sangha is ready, then for the benefit of these venerables and myself, I’ll confess in the midst of the Sangha both their and my own offenses by covering over as if with grass, except for heavy offenses and offenses connected with householders.

pli-tv-kd14:13.3.21.eng: Please, venerables, I ask the Sangha to listen.

pli-tv-kd14:13.3.22.pli: Amhākaṁ bhaṇḍanajātānaṁ kalahajātānaṁ vivādāpannānaṁ viharataṁ bahuṁ assāmaṇakaṁ ajjhāciṇṇaṁ bhāsitaparikkantaṁ.
pli-tv-kd14:13.3.22.eng: While we were arguing and disputing, we did and said many things unworthy of monastics.

pli-tv-kd14:13.3.23.pli: Sace mayaṁ imāhi āpattīhi aññamaññaṁ kāressāma, siyāpi taṁ adhikaraṇaṁ kakkhaḷattāya vāḷattāya bhedāya saṁvatteyya.
pli-tv-kd14:13.3.23.eng: If we deal with one another for these offenses, this legal issue might lead to harshness, nastiness, and schism.

pli-tv-kd14:13.3.24.pli: Ahaṁ yā ceva imesaṁ āyasmantānaṁ āpatti, yā ca attano āpatti, imesañceva āyasmantānaṁ atthāya, attano ca atthāya, saṅghamajjhe tiṇavatthārakena desemi, ṭhapetvā thullavajjaṁ, ṭhapetvā gihippaṭisaṁyuttaṁ.
pli-tv-kd14:13.3.24.eng: For the benefit of these venerables and myself, I confess in the midst of the Sangha both their and my own offenses by covering over as if with grass, except for heavy offenses and offenses connected with householders.

pli-tv-kd14:13.3.25.pli: Yassāyasmato khamati amhākaṁ imāsaṁ āpattīnaṁ saṅghamajjhe tiṇavatthārakena desanā, ṭhapetvā thullavajjaṁ, ṭhapetvā gihippaṭisaṁyuttaṁ, so tuṇhassa;
pli-tv-kd14:13.3.25.eng: Any monk who approves of confessing our offenses in the midst of the Sangha by covering over as if with grass should remain silent.

pli-tv-kd14:13.3.26.eng: Any monk who doesn’t approve should speak up.

pli-tv-kd14:13.3.27.pli: Desitā amhākaṁ imā āpattiyo saṅghamajjhe tiṇavatthārakena, ṭhapetvā thullavajjaṁ, ṭhapetvā gihippaṭisaṁyuttaṁ.
pli-tv-kd14:13.3.27.eng: We have confessed our offenses in the midst of the Sangha by covering over as if with grass, except for heavy offenses and offenses connected with householders.

pli-tv-kd14:13.3.28.pli: Khamati saṅghassa, tasmā tuṇhī, evametaṁ dhārayāmī’ti.
pli-tv-kd14:13.3.28.eng: The Sangha approves and is therefore silent. I’ll remember it thus.’

pli-tv-kd14:13.4.1.pli: Evañca pana, bhikkhave, te bhikkhū tāhi āpattīhi vuṭṭhitā honti, ṭhapetvā thullavajjaṁ, ṭhapetvā gihippaṭisaṁyuttaṁ, ṭhapetvā diṭṭhāvikammaṁ, ṭhapetvā ye na tattha hontī”ti.
pli-tv-kd14:13.4.1.eng: In this way those monks are cleared of those offenses, except for heavy offenses and offenses connected with householders, and except for those monks who voice their disapproval and those who are absent.”
pli-tv-kd14:13.4.1.comm: “Voice their disapproval” renders diṭṭhāvikamma, literally, “disclosure of view”. This cannot mean an objection made in the midst of the Sangha, for that would invalidate the procedure. Sp 4.214: Ye pana “na metaṁ khamatī”ti aññamaññaṁ diṭṭhāvikammaṁ karonti … te āpattīhi na vuṭṭhahanti, “But those who disclose their view to each other, saying, ‘I do not approve of this’ … they are not cleared of offenses.”

pli-tv-kd14:14.1.1.pli: Tena kho pana samayena bhikkhūpi bhikkhūhi vivadanti, bhikkhūpi bhikkhunīhi vivadanti, bhikkhuniyopi bhikkhūhi vivadanti, channopi bhikkhu bhikkhunīnaṁ anupakhajja bhikkhūhi saddhiṁ vivadati, bhikkhunīnaṁ pakkhaṁ gāheti.
pli-tv-kd14:14.1.1.eng: On one occasion monks were disputing with monks, monks with nuns, and nuns with monks. Standing with the nuns, the monk Channa disputed with the monks, making others side with the nuns.
pli-tv-kd14:14.1.1.comm: Bhikkhunīnaṁ anupakhajja, literally, “intruding on the nuns”. Sp 4.215: Bhikkhunīnaṁ anupakhajjāti bhikkhunīnaṁ anto pavisitvā, “Intruding on the nuns means having entered among the nuns.” The contextual point is that he is siding with the nuns.

pli-tv-kd14:14.1.2.pli: Ye te bhikkhū appicchā …pe… te ujjhāyanti khiyyanti vipācenti—
pli-tv-kd14:14.1.2.eng: The monks of few desires … complained and criticized him,

pli-tv-kd14:14.1.3.pli: “kathañhi nāma channo bhikkhu bhikkhunīnaṁ anupakhajja bhikkhūhi saddhiṁ vivadissati, bhikkhunīnaṁ pakkhaṁ gāhessatīti.
pli-tv-kd14:14.1.3.eng: “How can the monk Channa act like this?”

pli-tv-kd14:14.1.4.pli: Atha kho te bhikkhū bhagavato etamatthaṁ ārocesuṁ …pe…

pli-tv-kd14:14.1.5.eng: “Is it true, monks, that the monk Channa is acting like this?”

pli-tv-kd14:14.1.8.eng: the Buddha gave a teaching and addressed the monks:

pli-tv-kd14:14.2.1.pli: “Cattārimāni, bhikkhave, adhikaraṇāni— vivādādhikaraṇaṁ, anuvādādhikaraṇaṁ, āpattādhikaraṇaṁ, kiccādhikaraṇaṁ.
pli-tv-kd14:14.2.1.eng: “Monks, there are four kinds of legal issues: legal issues arising from disputes; legal issues arising from accusations; legal issues arising from offenses; legal issues arising from business.”

pli-tv-kd14:14.2.2.eng: “What’s a legal issue arising from a dispute?

pli-tv-kd14:14.2.3.eng: It may be that the monks are disputing, saying,

pli-tv-kd14:14.2.4.pli: dhammoti vā adhammoti vā, vinayoti vā avinayoti vā, bhāsitaṁ lapitaṁ tathāgatenāti vā abhāsitaṁ alapitaṁ tathāgatenāti vā, āciṇṇaṁ tathāgatenāti vā anāciṇṇaṁ tathāgatenāti vā, paññattaṁ tathāgatenāti vā apaññattaṁ tathāgatenāti vā, āpattīti vā anāpattīti vā, lahukā āpattīti vā garukā āpattīti vā, sāvasesā āpattīti vā anavasesā āpattīti vā, duṭṭhullā āpattīti vā aduṭṭhullā āpattīti vā?
pli-tv-kd14:14.2.4.eng: ‘This is the Teaching’, ‘This is contrary to the Teaching’, ‘This is the Monastic Law’, ‘This is contrary to the Monastic Law’, ‘This was spoken by the Buddha’, ‘This wasn’t spoken by the Buddha’, ‘This was practiced by the Buddha’, ‘This wasn’t practiced by the Buddha’, ‘This was laid down by the Buddha’, ‘This wasn’t laid down by the Buddha’, ‘This is an offense’, ‘This isn’t an offense’, ‘This is a light offense’, ‘This is a heavy offense’, ‘This is a curable offense’, ‘This is an incurable offense’, ‘This is a grave offense’, or ‘This is a minor offense.’

pli-tv-kd14:14.2.5.pli: Yaṁ tattha bhaṇḍanaṁ kalaho viggaho vivādo nānāvādo aññathāvādo vipaccatāya vohāro medhagaṁ—
pli-tv-kd14:14.2.5.eng: In regard to this, whatever there is of quarreling, arguing, conflict, disputing, variety in opinion, difference in opinion, heated speech, or strife—

pli-tv-kd14:14.2.6.eng: this is called a legal issue arising from a dispute.

pli-tv-kd14:14.2.7.eng: What’s a legal issue arising from an accusation?

pli-tv-kd14:14.2.8.pli: Idha pana, bhikkhave, bhikkhū bhikkhuṁ anuvadanti sīlavipattiyā vā ācāravipattiyā vā diṭṭhivipattiyā vā ājīvavipattiyā vā.
pli-tv-kd14:14.2.8.eng: It may be that the monks accuse a monk of failure in morality, failure in conduct, failure in view, or failure in livelihood.

pli-tv-kd14:14.2.9.pli: Yo tattha anuvādo anuvadanā anullapanā anubhaṇanā anusampavaṅkatā abbhussahanatā anubalappadānaṁ—
pli-tv-kd14:14.2.9.eng: In regard to this, whatever there is of accusations, accusing, allegations, blame, taking sides because of friendship, taking part in the accusation, or supporting the accusation—

pli-tv-kd14:14.2.10.eng: this is called a legal issue arising from an accusation.

pli-tv-kd14:14.2.11.eng: What’s a legal issue arising from an offense?

pli-tv-kd14:14.2.12.pli: Pañcapi āpattikkhandhā āpattādhikaraṇaṁ, sattapi āpattikkhandhā āpattādhikaraṇaṁ—
pli-tv-kd14:14.2.12.eng: There are legal issues arising from offenses because of the five classes of offenses; there are legal issues arising from offenses because of the seven classes of offenses—

pli-tv-kd14:14.2.13.eng: this is called a legal issue arising from an offense.

pli-tv-kd14:14.2.14.eng: What’s a legal issue arising from business?

pli-tv-kd14:14.2.15.pli: Yā saṅghassa kiccayatā, karaṇīyatā, apalokanakammaṁ, ñattikammaṁ, ñattidutiyakammaṁ, ñatticatutthakammaṁ—
pli-tv-kd14:14.2.15.eng: Whatever is the duty or the business of the Sangha—a legal procedure consisting of getting permission, a legal procedure consisting of one motion, a legal procedure consisting of one motion and one announcement, a legal procedure consisting of one motion and three announcements—

pli-tv-kd14:14.2.16.eng: this is called a legal issue arising from business.”

pli-tv-kd14:14.3.0.eng: The roots of legal issues arising from disputes

pli-tv-kd14:14.3.1.eng: “What’s the root of legal issues arising from disputes?

pli-tv-kd14:14.3.2.eng: There are six roots of disputes that in turn are the root of legal issues arising from disputes.

pli-tv-kd14:14.3.3.pli: Tīṇipi akusalamūlāni vivādādhikaraṇassa mūlaṁ, tīṇipi kusalamūlāni vivādādhikaraṇassa mūlaṁ.
pli-tv-kd14:14.3.3.eng: There are also three unwholesome and three wholesome roots of legal issues arising from disputes.

pli-tv-kd14:14.3.4.eng: What are the six roots of disputes that in turn are the root of legal issues arising from disputes?

pli-tv-kd14:14.3.5.pli: Idha pana, bhikkhave, bhikkhu kodhano hoti upanāhī.
pli-tv-kd14:14.3.5.eng: It may be that a monk is angry and resentful.

pli-tv-kd14:14.3.6.pli: Yo so, bhikkhave, bhikkhu kodhano hoti upanāhī, so sattharipi agāravo viharati appatisso, dhammepi agāravo viharati appatisso, saṅghepi agāravo viharati appatisso, sikkhāyapi na paripūrakārī hoti.
pli-tv-kd14:14.3.6.eng: One who’s angry and resentful is disrespectful and undeferential toward the Teacher, the Teaching, and the Sangha, and he doesn’t fulfill the training.

pli-tv-kd14:14.3.7.pli: Yo so, bhikkhave, bhikkhu sattharipi agāravo viharati appatisso, dhammepi …pe…

pli-tv-kd14:14.3.9.pli: sikkhāyapi na paripūrakārī, so saṅghe vivādaṁ janeti.

pli-tv-kd14:14.3.10.pli: Yo hoti vivādo bahujanāhitāya bahujanāsukhāya bahuno janassa anatthāya ahitāya dukkhāya devamanussānaṁ.
pli-tv-kd14:14.3.10.eng: Disputes are unbeneficial and a cause of unhappiness for humanity; they’re harmful, detrimental, and a cause of suffering for gods and humans.

pli-tv-kd14:14.3.11.pli: Evarūpañce tumhe, bhikkhave, vivādamūlaṁ ajjhattaṁ vā bahiddhā vā samanupasseyyātha, tatra tumhe, bhikkhave, tasseva pāpakassa vivādamūlassa pahānāya vāyameyyātha.
pli-tv-kd14:14.3.11.eng: Monks, when you see such a root of disputes either in yourself or others, you should make an effort to get rid of it.

pli-tv-kd14:14.3.12.pli: Evarūpañce tumhe, bhikkhave, vivādamūlaṁ ajjhattaṁ vā bahiddhā vā na samanupasseyyātha, tatra tumhe, bhikkhave, tasseva pāpakassa vivādamūlassa āyatiṁ anavassavāya paṭipajjeyyātha.
pli-tv-kd14:14.3.12.eng: If you don’t see such a root either in yourself or others, you should practice so that it doesn’t emerge in the future.

pli-tv-kd14:14.3.13.eng: In this way, that bad root of disputes is abandoned

pli-tv-kd14:14.3.14.pli: Evametassa pāpakassa vivādamūlassa āyatiṁ anavassavo hoti.
pli-tv-kd14:14.3.14.eng: and doesn’t emerge in the future.

pli-tv-kd14:14.3.15.pli: Puna caparaṁ, bhikkhave, bhikkhu makkhī hoti paḷāsī …pe…
pli-tv-kd14:14.3.15.eng: Or it may be that a monk is denigrating and domineering,

pli-tv-kd14:14.3.16.pli: issukī hoti maccharī, saṭho hoti māyāvī, pāpiccho hoti micchādiṭṭhī, sandiṭṭhiparāmāsī hoti ādhānaggāhī duppaṭinissaggī.
pli-tv-kd14:14.3.16.eng: envious and stingy, treacherous and deceitful, one who has bad desires and wrong views, or one who obstinately grasps his own views and only gives them up with difficulty.

pli-tv-kd14:14.3.17.pli: Yo so, bhikkhave, bhikkhu sandiṭṭhiparāmāsī hoti ādhānaggāhī duppaṭinissaggī, so sattharipi agāravo viharati appatisso, dhammepi agāravo viharati appatisso, saṅghepi agāravo viharati appatisso, sikkhāyapi na paripūrakārī hoti.
pli-tv-kd14:14.3.17.eng: Such a monk is disrespectful and undeferential toward the Teacher, the Teaching, and the Sangha, and he doesn’t fulfill the training.

pli-tv-kd14:14.3.18.pli: Yo so, bhikkhave, bhikkhu sattharipi agāravo viharati appatisso, dhammepi …pe…

pli-tv-kd14:14.3.20.pli: sikkhāyapi na paripūrakārī, so saṅghe vivādaṁ janeti.

pli-tv-kd14:14.3.21.pli: Yo hoti vivādo bahujanāhitāya bahujanāsukhāya bahuno janassa anatthāya ahitāya dukkhāya devamanussānaṁ.
pli-tv-kd14:14.3.21.eng: Disputes are unbeneficial and a cause of unhappiness for humanity; they’re harmful, detrimental, and a cause of suffering for gods and humans.

pli-tv-kd14:14.3.22.pli: Evarūpañce tumhe, bhikkhave, vivādamūlaṁ ajjhattaṁ vā bahiddhā vā samanupasseyyātha, tatra tumhe, bhikkhave, tasseva pāpakassa vivādamūlassa pahānāya vāyameyyātha.
pli-tv-kd14:14.3.22.eng: Monks, when you see such a root of disputes either in yourself or others, you should make an effort to get rid of it.

pli-tv-kd14:14.3.23.pli: Evarūpañce tumhe, bhikkhave, vivādamūlaṁ ajjhattaṁ vā bahiddhā vā na samanupasseyyātha, tatra tumhe, bhikkhave, tasseva pāpakassa vivādamūlassa āyatiṁ anavassavāya paṭipajjeyyātha.
pli-tv-kd14:14.3.23.eng: If you don’t see such a root either in yourself or others, you should practice so that it doesn’t emerge in the future.

pli-tv-kd14:14.3.24.eng: In this way that bad root of disputes is abandoned

pli-tv-kd14:14.3.25.pli: Evametassa pāpakassa vivādamūlassa āyatiṁ anavassavo hoti.
pli-tv-kd14:14.3.25.eng: and doesn’t emerge in the future.

pli-tv-kd14:14.4.1.eng: What are the three unwholesome roots of legal issues arising from disputes?

pli-tv-kd14:14.4.2.pli: Idha pana, bhikkhave, bhikkhū luddhacittā vivadanti, duṭṭhacittā vivadanti, mūḷhacittā vivadanti—
pli-tv-kd14:14.4.2.eng: It may be that monks dispute with a mind of greed, ill will, or delusion, saying,

pli-tv-kd14:14.4.3.pli: dhammoti vā adhammoti vā, vinayoti vā avinayoti vā, bhāsitaṁ lapitaṁ tathāgatenāti vā abhāsitaṁ alapitaṁ tathāgatenāti vā, āciṇṇaṁ tathāgatenāti vā anāciṇṇaṁ tathāgatenāti vā, paññattaṁ tathāgatenāti vā apaññattaṁ tathāgatenāti vā, āpattīti vā anāpattīti vā, lahukā āpattīti vā garukā āpattīti vā, sāvasesā āpattīti vā anavasesā āpattīti vā, duṭṭhullā āpattīti vā aduṭṭhullā āpattīti vā.
pli-tv-kd14:14.4.3.eng: ‘This is the Teaching’, ‘This is contrary to the Teaching’, ‘This is the Monastic Law’, ‘This is contrary to the Monastic Law’, ‘This was spoken by the Buddha’, ‘This wasn’t spoken by the Buddha’, ‘This was practiced by the Buddha’, ‘This wasn’t practiced by the Buddha’, ‘This was laid down by the Buddha’, ‘This wasn’t laid down by the Buddha’, ‘This is an offense’, ‘This isn’t an offense’, ‘This is a light offense’, ‘This is a heavy offense’, ‘This is a curable offense’, ‘This is an incurable offense’, ‘This is a grave offense’, or ‘This is a minor offense.’

pli-tv-kd14:14.4.5.eng: What are the three wholesome roots of legal issues arising from disputes?

pli-tv-kd14:14.4.6.pli: Idha pana, bhikkhave, bhikkhū aluddhacittā vivadanti, aduṭṭhacittā vivadanti, amūḷhacittā vivadanti—
pli-tv-kd14:14.4.6.eng: It may be that monks dispute with a mind free from greed, ill will, and delusion, saying,

pli-tv-kd14:14.4.7.eng: ‘This is the Teaching’, ‘This is contrary to the Teaching’ …

pli-tv-kd14:14.4.8.pli: duṭṭhullā āpattīti vā aduṭṭhullā āpattīti vā.
pli-tv-kd14:14.4.8.eng: ‘This is a grave offense’, or ‘This is a minor offense.’”

pli-tv-kd14:14.5.0.eng: The roots of legal issues arising from accusations

pli-tv-kd14:14.5.1.eng: “What’s the root of legal issues arising from accusations?

pli-tv-kd14:14.5.2.eng: There are six roots of accusations that in turn are the root of legal issues arising from accusations.

pli-tv-kd14:14.5.3.pli: Tīṇipi akusalamūlāni anuvādādhikaraṇassa mūlaṁ, tīṇipi kusalamūlāni anuvādādhikaraṇassa mūlaṁ, kāyopi anuvādādhikaraṇassa mūlaṁ, vācāpi anuvādādhikaraṇassa mūlaṁ.
pli-tv-kd14:14.5.3.eng: There are also three unwholesome and three wholesome roots of legal issues arising from accusations. The body, too, is a root of legal issues arising from accusations, as is speech.

pli-tv-kd14:14.5.4.eng: What are the six roots of accusations that in turn are the root of legal issues arising from accusations?

pli-tv-kd14:14.5.5.pli: Idha pana, bhikkhave, bhikkhu kodhano hoti upanāhī.
pli-tv-kd14:14.5.5.eng: It may be that a monk is angry and resentful.

pli-tv-kd14:14.5.6.pli: Yo so, bhikkhave, bhikkhu kodhano hoti upanāhī, so sattharipi agāravo viharati appatisso, dhammepi agāravo viharati appatisso, saṅghepi agāravo viharati appatisso, sikkhāyapi na paripūrakārī hoti.
pli-tv-kd14:14.5.6.eng: One who is angry and resentful is disrespectful and undeferential toward the Teacher, the Teaching, and the Sangha, and he doesn’t fulfill the training.

pli-tv-kd14:14.5.7.pli: Yo so, bhikkhave, bhikkhu sattharipi agāravo viharati appatisso, dhammepi …pe…

pli-tv-kd14:14.5.9.pli: sikkhāyapi na paripūrakārī, so saṅghe anuvādaṁ janeti.

pli-tv-kd14:14.5.10.pli: Yo hoti anuvādo bahujanāhitāya bahujanāsukhāya bahuno janassa anatthāya ahitāya dukkhāya devamanussānaṁ.
pli-tv-kd14:14.5.10.eng: Accusations are unbeneficial and a cause of unhappiness for humanity; they’re harmful, detrimental, and a cause of suffering for gods and humans.

pli-tv-kd14:14.5.11.pli: Evarūpañce tumhe, bhikkhave, anuvādamūlaṁ ajjhattaṁ vā bahiddhā vā samanupasseyyātha, tatra tumhe, bhikkhave, tasseva pāpakassa anuvādamūlassa pahānāya vāyameyyātha.
pli-tv-kd14:14.5.11.eng: Monks, when you see such a root of accusations either in yourself or others, you should make an effort to get rid of it.

pli-tv-kd14:14.5.12.pli: Evarūpañce tumhe, bhikkhave, anuvādamūlaṁ ajjhattaṁ vā bahiddhā vā na samanupasseyyātha, tatra tumhe, bhikkhave, tasseva pāpakassa anuvādamūlassa āyatiṁ anavassavāya paṭipajjeyyātha.
pli-tv-kd14:14.5.12.eng: If you don’t see such a root either in yourself or others, you should practice so that it doesn’t emerge in the future.

pli-tv-kd14:14.5.14.pli: Evametassa pāpakassa anuvādamūlassa āyatiṁ anavassavo hoti.

pli-tv-kd14:14.5.15.pli: Puna caparaṁ, bhikkhave, bhikkhu makkhī hoti paḷāsī …pe…
pli-tv-kd14:14.5.15.eng: Or it may be that a monk is denigrating and domineering,

pli-tv-kd14:14.5.16.pli: issukī hoti maccharī, saṭho hoti māyāvī, pāpiccho hoti micchādiṭṭhī, sandiṭṭhiparāmāsī hoti ādhānaggāhī duppaṭinissaggī.
pli-tv-kd14:14.5.16.eng: envious and stingy, treacherous and deceitful, one who has bad desires and wrong views, or one who obstinately grasps his own views and only gives them up with difficulty.

pli-tv-kd14:14.5.17.pli: Yo so, bhikkhave, bhikkhu sandiṭṭhiparāmāsī hoti ādhānaggāhī duppaṭinissaggī, so sattharipi agāravo viharati appatisso, dhammepi agāravo viharati appatisso, saṅghepi agāravo viharati appatisso, sikkhāyapi na paripūrakārī hoti.
pli-tv-kd14:14.5.17.eng: Such a monk is disrespectful and undeferential toward the Teacher, the Teaching, and the Sangha, and he doesn’t fulfill the training.

pli-tv-kd14:14.5.18.pli: Yo so, bhikkhave, bhikkhu sattharipi agāravo viharati appatisso, dhammepi …pe…

pli-tv-kd14:14.5.20.pli: sikkhāyapi na paripūrakārī, so saṅghe anuvādaṁ janeti.

pli-tv-kd14:14.5.21.pli: Yo hoti anuvādo bahujanāhitāya bahujanāsukhāya bahuno janassa anatthāya ahitāya dukkhāya devamanussānaṁ.
pli-tv-kd14:14.5.21.eng: Accusations are unbeneficial and a cause of unhappiness for humanity; they’re harmful, detrimental, and a cause of suffering for gods and humans.

pli-tv-kd14:14.5.22.pli: Evarūpañce tumhe, bhikkhave, anuvādamūlaṁ ajjhattaṁ vā bahiddhā vā samanupasseyyātha, tatra tumhe, bhikkhave, tasseva pāpakassa anuvādamūlassa pahānāya vāyameyyātha.
pli-tv-kd14:14.5.22.eng: Monks, when you see such a root of accusations either in yourself or others, you should make an effort to get rid of it.

pli-tv-kd14:14.5.23.pli: Evarūpañce tumhe, bhikkhave, anuvādamūlaṁ ajjhattaṁ vā bahiddhā vā na samanupasseyyātha, tatra tumhe, bhikkhave, tasseva pāpakassa anuvādamūlassa āyatiṁ anavassavāya paṭipajjeyyātha.
pli-tv-kd14:14.5.23.eng: If you don’t see such a root either in yourself or others, you should practice so that it doesn’t emerge in the future.

pli-tv-kd14:14.5.25.pli: Evametassa pāpakassa anuvādamūlassa āyatiṁ anavassavo hoti.

pli-tv-kd14:14.5.27.eng: What are the three unwholesome roots of accusations?

pli-tv-kd14:14.5.28.pli: Idha pana, bhikkhave, bhikkhū bhikkhuṁ luddhacittā anuvadanti, duṭṭhacittā anuvadanti, mūḷhacittā anuvadanti—
pli-tv-kd14:14.5.28.eng: It may be that monks, because of greed, ill will, or delusion, accuse a monk of

pli-tv-kd14:14.5.29.pli: sīlavipattiyā vā, ācāravipattiyā vā, diṭṭhivipattiyā vā, ājīvavipattiyā vā.
pli-tv-kd14:14.5.29.eng: failure in morality, conduct, view, or livelihood.

pli-tv-kd14:14.5.31.eng: What are the three wholesome roots of accusations?

pli-tv-kd14:14.5.32.pli: Idha pana, bhikkhave, bhikkhū bhikkhuṁ aluddhacittā anuvadanti, aduṭṭhacittā anuvadanti, amūḷhacittā anuvadanti—
pli-tv-kd14:14.5.32.eng: It may be that monks, because of non-greed, non-ill will, and non-delusion, accuse a monk of

pli-tv-kd14:14.5.33.pli: sīlavipattiyā vā, ācāravipattiyā vā, diṭṭhivipattiyā vā, ājīvavipattiyā vā.
pli-tv-kd14:14.5.33.eng: failure in morality, conduct, view, or livelihood.

pli-tv-kd14:14.5.35.eng: How’s the body a root of legal issues arising from accusations?

pli-tv-kd14:14.5.36.pli: Idhekacco dubbaṇṇo hoti, duddassiko, okoṭimako, bahvābādho, kāṇo vā, kuṇī vā, khañjo vā, pakkhahato vā, yena naṁ anuvadanti.
pli-tv-kd14:14.5.36.eng: It may be that someone is ugly, unsightly, a dwarf, sickly, blind in one eye, crooked-limbed, lame, or paralyzed, and they blame him for that.

pli-tv-kd14:14.5.38.eng: How’s speech a root of legal issues arising from accusations?

pli-tv-kd14:14.5.39.pli: Idhekacco dubbaco hoti, mammano, eḷagalavāco, yāya naṁ anuvadanti.
pli-tv-kd14:14.5.39.eng: It may be that someone is difficult to correct, or he stutters or dribbles while speaking, and they blame him for that.”

pli-tv-kd14:14.6.0.eng: The roots of legal issues arising from offenses and business

pli-tv-kd14:14.6.1.eng: “What’s the root of legal issues arising from offenses?

pli-tv-kd14:14.6.2.eng: There are six originations of offenses that in turn are the root of legal issues arising from offenses.

pli-tv-kd14:14.6.3.pli: Atthāpatti kāyato samuṭṭhāti, na vācato, na cittato.
pli-tv-kd14:14.6.3.eng: There are offenses that originate from the body, but not from speech or the mind.

pli-tv-kd14:14.6.4.pli: Atthāpatti vācato samuṭṭhāti, na kāyato, na cittato.
pli-tv-kd14:14.6.4.eng: There are offenses that originate from speech, but not from the body or the mind.

pli-tv-kd14:14.6.5.pli: Atthāpatti kāyato ca vācato ca samuṭṭhāti, na cittato.
pli-tv-kd14:14.6.5.eng: There are offenses that originate from the body and speech, but not from the mind.

pli-tv-kd14:14.6.6.pli: Atthāpatti kāyato ca cittato ca samuṭṭhāti, na vācato.
pli-tv-kd14:14.6.6.eng: There are offenses that originate from the body and the mind, but not from speech.

pli-tv-kd14:14.6.7.pli: Atthāpatti vācato ca cittato ca samuṭṭhāti, na kāyato.
pli-tv-kd14:14.6.7.eng: There are offenses that originate from speech and the mind, but not from the body.

pli-tv-kd14:14.6.8.pli: Atthāpatti kāyato ca vācato ca cittato ca samuṭṭhāti.
pli-tv-kd14:14.6.8.eng: There are offenses that originate from the body, speech, and the mind.

pli-tv-kd14:14.7.1.eng: What’s the root of legal issues arising from business?

pli-tv-kd14:14.7.2.eng: There’s one root of legal issues arising from business:

pli-tv-kd14:14.8.0.eng: Ethical qualities of legal issues arising from disputes

pli-tv-kd14:14.8.1.eng: “Is a legal issue arising from a dispute wholesome, unwholesome, or indeterminate?
pli-tv-kd14:14.8.1.comm: According to the sub-commentary, the Pali should be understood as a question. Sp-ṭ 4.220: Vivādādhikaraṇaṁ kusalaṁ akusalaṁ abyākatanti vivādādhikaraṇaṁ kiṁ kusalaṁ akusalaṁ udāhu abyākatanti pucchati. Vivādādhikaraṇaṁ siyā kusalantiādi vissajjanaṁ. Esa nayo sesesupi, “Vivādādhikaraṇaṁ kusalaṁ akusalaṁ abyākatan: he asks, ‘Is a legal issue arising from a dispute wholesome, unwholesome, or indeterminate?’ ‘A legal issue arising from a dispute may be wholesome,’ etc., is the response. This method also applies to the rest (below).”

pli-tv-kd14:14.8.2.pli: Vivādādhikaraṇaṁ siyā kusalaṁ, siyā akusalaṁ, siyā abyākataṁ.
pli-tv-kd14:14.8.2.eng: A legal issue arising from a dispute may be wholesome, unwholesome, or indeterminate.

pli-tv-kd14:14.8.3.eng: What’s a wholesome legal issue arising from a dispute?

pli-tv-kd14:14.8.4.pli: Idha pana, bhikkhave, bhikkhū kusalacittā vivadanti—
pli-tv-kd14:14.8.4.eng: It may be that monks dispute with a wholesome mind, saying,

pli-tv-kd14:14.8.5.eng: ‘This is the Teaching’, ‘This is contrary to the Teaching’ …

pli-tv-kd14:14.8.6.pli: duṭṭhullā āpattīti vā aduṭṭhullā āpattīti vā.
pli-tv-kd14:14.8.6.eng: ‘This is a grave offense’, or ‘This is a minor offense.’

pli-tv-kd14:14.8.7.pli: Yaṁ tattha bhaṇḍanaṁ kalaho viggaho vivādo nānāvādo aññathāvādo vipaccatāya vohāro medhagaṁ—
pli-tv-kd14:14.8.7.eng: In regard to this, whatever there is of quarreling, arguing, conflict, disputing, variety in opinion, difference in opinion, heated speech, or strife—

pli-tv-kd14:14.8.8.eng: this is called a wholesome legal issue arising from a dispute.

pli-tv-kd14:14.8.9.eng: What’s an unwholesome legal issue arising from a dispute?

pli-tv-kd14:14.8.10.pli: Idha pana, bhikkhave, bhikkhū akusalacittā vivadanti—
pli-tv-kd14:14.8.10.eng: It may be that monks dispute with an unwholesome mind, saying,

pli-tv-kd14:14.8.11.eng: ‘This is the Teaching’, ‘This is contrary to the Teaching’ …

pli-tv-kd14:14.8.12.pli: duṭṭhullā āpattīti vā aduṭṭhullā āpattīti vā.
pli-tv-kd14:14.8.12.eng: ‘This is a grave offense’, or ‘This is a minor offense.’

pli-tv-kd14:14.8.13.pli: Yaṁ tattha bhaṇḍanaṁ kalaho viggaho vivādo nānāvādo aññathāvādo vipaccatāya vohāro medhagaṁ—
pli-tv-kd14:14.8.13.eng: In regard to this, whatever there is of quarreling, arguing, conflict, disputing, variety in opinion, difference in opinion, heated speech, or strife—

pli-tv-kd14:14.8.14.eng: this is called an unwholesome legal issue arising from a dispute.

pli-tv-kd14:14.8.15.eng: What’s an indeterminate legal issue arising from a dispute?

pli-tv-kd14:14.8.16.pli: Idha pana, bhikkhave, bhikkhū abyākatacittā vivadanti—
pli-tv-kd14:14.8.16.eng: It may be that monks dispute with an indeterminate mind, saying,

pli-tv-kd14:14.8.17.eng: ‘This is the Teaching’, ‘This is contrary to the Teaching’ …

pli-tv-kd14:14.8.18.pli: duṭṭhullā āpattīti vā aduṭṭhullā āpattīti vā.
pli-tv-kd14:14.8.18.eng: ‘This is a grave offense’, or ‘This is a minor offense.’

pli-tv-kd14:14.8.19.pli: Yaṁ tattha bhaṇḍanaṁ kalaho viggaho vivādo nānāvādo aññathāvādo vipaccatāya vohāro medhagaṁ—
pli-tv-kd14:14.8.19.eng: In regard to this, whatever there is of quarreling, arguing, conflict, disputing, variety in opinion, difference in opinion, heated speech, or strife—

pli-tv-kd14:14.8.20.eng: this is called an indeterminate legal issue arising from a dispute.”

pli-tv-kd14:14.9.0.eng: Ethical qualities of legal issues arising from accusations

pli-tv-kd14:14.9.1.eng: “Is a legal issue arising from an accusation wholesome, unwholesome, or indeterminate?

pli-tv-kd14:14.9.2.pli: Anuvādādhikaraṇaṁ siyā kusalaṁ, siyā akusalaṁ, siyā abyākataṁ.
pli-tv-kd14:14.9.2.eng: A legal issue arising from an accusation may be wholesome, unwholesome, or indeterminate.

pli-tv-kd14:14.9.3.eng: What’s a wholesome legal issue arising from an accusation?

pli-tv-kd14:14.9.4.pli: Idha pana, bhikkhave, bhikkhū bhikkhuṁ kusalacittā anuvadanti—
pli-tv-kd14:14.9.4.eng: It may be that monks with wholesome minds accuse a monk

pli-tv-kd14:14.9.5.pli: sīlavipattiyā vā, ācāravipattiyā vā, diṭṭhivipattiyā vā, ājīvavipattiyā vā.
pli-tv-kd14:14.9.5.eng: of failure in morality, conduct, view, or livelihood.

pli-tv-kd14:14.9.6.pli: Yo tattha anuvādo anuvadanā anullapanā anubhaṇanā anusampavaṅkatā abbhussahanatā anubalappadānaṁ—
pli-tv-kd14:14.9.6.eng: In regard to this, whatever there is of accusations, accusing, allegations, blame, taking sides because of friendship, taking part in the accusation, or supporting the accusation—

pli-tv-kd14:14.9.7.eng: this is called a wholesome legal issue arising from an accusation.

pli-tv-kd14:14.9.8.eng: What’s an unwholesome legal issue arising from an accusation?

pli-tv-kd14:14.9.9.pli: Idha pana, bhikkhave, bhikkhū bhikkhuṁ akusalacittā anuvadanti—
pli-tv-kd14:14.9.9.eng: It may be that monks with unwholesome minds accuse a monk

pli-tv-kd14:14.9.10.pli: sīlavipattiyā vā, ācāravipattiyā vā, diṭṭhivipattiyā vā, ājīvavipattiyā vā.
pli-tv-kd14:14.9.10.eng: of failure in morality,  conduct, view, or livelihood.

pli-tv-kd14:14.9.11.pli: Yo tattha anuvādo anuvadanā anullapanā anubhaṇanā anusampavaṅkatā abbhussahanatā anubalappadānaṁ—
pli-tv-kd14:14.9.11.eng: In regard to this, whatever there is of accusations, accusing, allegations, blame, taking sides because of friendship, taking part in the accusation, or supporting the accusation—

pli-tv-kd14:14.9.12.eng: this is called an unwholesome legal issue arising from an accusation.

pli-tv-kd14:14.9.13.eng: What’s an indeterminate legal issue arising from an accusation?

pli-tv-kd14:14.9.14.pli: Idha pana, bhikkhave, bhikkhū bhikkhuṁ abyākatacittā anuvadanti—
pli-tv-kd14:14.9.14.eng: It may be that monks with indeterminate minds accuse a monk

pli-tv-kd14:14.9.15.pli: sīlavipattiyā vā, ācāravipattiyā vā, diṭṭhivipattiyā vā, ājīvavipattiyā vā.
pli-tv-kd14:14.9.15.eng: of failure in morality, conduct, view, or livelihood.

pli-tv-kd14:14.9.16.pli: Yo tattha anuvādo anuvadanā anullapanā anubhaṇanā anusampavaṅkatā abbhussahanatā anubalappadānaṁ—
pli-tv-kd14:14.9.16.eng: In regard to this, whatever there is of accusations, accusing, allegations, blame, taking sides because of friendship, taking part in the accusation, or supporting the accusation—

pli-tv-kd14:14.9.17.eng: this is called an indeterminate legal issue arising from an accusation.”

pli-tv-kd14:14.9.18.eng: “Is a legal issue arising from an offense wholesome, unwholesome, or indeterminate?

pli-tv-kd14:14.10.0.eng: Ethical qualities of legal issues arising from offenses

pli-tv-kd14:14.10.1.eng: A legal issue arising from an offense may be unwholesome or indeterminate.

pli-tv-kd14:14.10.2.eng: There are no wholesome legal issues arising from an offense.

pli-tv-kd14:14.10.3.eng: What’s an unwholesome legal issue arising from an offense?

pli-tv-kd14:14.10.4.pli: Yaṁ jānanto sañjānanto cecca abhivitaritvā vītikkamo—
pli-tv-kd14:14.10.4.eng: When one transgresses, knowing, perceiving, having intended, having decided—

pli-tv-kd14:14.10.5.eng: this is called an unwholesome legal issue arising from an offense.

pli-tv-kd14:14.10.6.eng: What’s an indeterminate legal issue arising from an offense?

pli-tv-kd14:14.10.7.pli: Yaṁ ajānanto asañjānanto acecca anabhivitaritvā vītikkamo—
pli-tv-kd14:14.10.7.eng: When one transgresses, not knowing, not perceiving, not having intended, not having decided—

pli-tv-kd14:14.10.8.eng: this is called an indeterminate legal issue arising from an offense.”

pli-tv-kd14:14.11.0.eng: Ethical qualities of legal issues arising from business

pli-tv-kd14:14.11.1.eng: “Is a legal issue arising from business wholesome, unwholesome, or indeterminate?

pli-tv-kd14:14.11.2.pli: Kiccādhikaraṇaṁ siyā kusalaṁ, siyā akusalaṁ, siyā abyākataṁ.
pli-tv-kd14:14.11.2.eng: A legal issue arising from business may be wholesome, unwholesome, or indeterminate.

pli-tv-kd14:14.11.3.eng: What’s a wholesome legal issue arising from business?

pli-tv-kd14:14.11.4.eng: When the Sangha does a legal procedure with a wholesome mind—

pli-tv-kd14:14.11.5.eng: whether a procedure consisting of getting permission, a procedure consisting of one motion, a procedure consisting of one motion and one announcement, or a procedure consisting of one motion and three announcements—

pli-tv-kd14:14.11.6.eng: this is called a wholesome legal issue arising from business.

pli-tv-kd14:14.11.7.eng: What’s an unwholesome legal issue arising from business?

pli-tv-kd14:14.11.8.eng: When the Sangha does a legal procedure with an unwholesome mind—

pli-tv-kd14:14.11.9.eng: whether a procedure consisting of getting permission, a procedure consisting of one motion, a procedure consisting of one motion and one announcement, or a procedure consisting of one motion and three announcements—

pli-tv-kd14:14.11.10.eng: this is called an unwholesome legal issue arising from business.

pli-tv-kd14:14.11.11.eng: What’s an indeterminate legal issue arising from business?

pli-tv-kd14:14.11.12.eng: When the Sangha does a legal procedure with an indeterminate mind—

pli-tv-kd14:14.11.13.eng: whether a procedure consisting of getting permission, a procedure consisting of one motion, a procedure consisting of one motion and one announcement, or a procedure consisting of one motion and three announcements—

pli-tv-kd14:14.11.14.eng: this is called an indeterminate legal issue arising from business.”

pli-tv-kd14:14.12.0.eng: Relationship between disputes and legal issues

pli-tv-kd14:14.12.1.pli: Vivādo vivādādhikaraṇaṁ, vivādo no adhikaraṇaṁ, adhikaraṇaṁ no vivādo, adhikaraṇañceva vivādo ca.
pli-tv-kd14:14.12.1.eng: “Are there disputes that are also legal issues arising from a dispute? Are there disputes that aren’t also legal issues? Are there legal issues that aren’t also disputes? Are there legal issues that are also disputes?

pli-tv-kd14:14.12.2.pli: Siyā vivādo vivādādhikaraṇaṁ, siyā vivādo no adhikaraṇaṁ, siyā adhikaraṇaṁ no vivādo, siyā adhikaraṇañceva vivādo ca.
pli-tv-kd14:14.12.2.eng: There are disputes that are also legal issues arising from a dispute. There are disputes that aren’t also legal issues. There are legal issues that aren’t also disputes. There are legal issues that are also disputes.

pli-tv-kd14:14.12.3.eng: How’s there a dispute that’s also a legal issue arising from a dispute?

pli-tv-kd14:14.12.4.eng: It may be that monks are disputing, saying,

pli-tv-kd14:14.12.5.eng: ‘This is the Teaching’, ‘This is contrary to the Teaching’ …

pli-tv-kd14:14.12.6.pli: duṭṭhullā āpattīti vā aduṭṭhullā āpattīti vā.
pli-tv-kd14:14.12.6.eng: ‘This is a grave offense’, or ‘This is a minor offense.’

pli-tv-kd14:14.12.7.pli: Yaṁ tattha bhaṇḍanaṁ kalaho viggaho vivādo nānāvādo aññathāvādo vipaccatāya vohāro medhagaṁ—
pli-tv-kd14:14.12.7.eng: In regard to this, whatever there is of quarreling, arguing, conflict, disputing, variety in opinion, difference in opinion, heated speech, or strife—

pli-tv-kd14:14.12.8.eng: this is a dispute that’s also a legal issue arising from a dispute.

pli-tv-kd14:14.12.9.eng: How’s there a dispute that isn’t also a legal issue?

pli-tv-kd14:14.12.10.pli: Mātāpi puttena vivadati, puttopi mātarā vivadati, pitāpi puttena vivadati, puttopi pitarā vivadati, bhātāpi bhātarā vivadati, bhātāpi bhaginiyā vivadati, bhaginīpi bhātarā vivadati, sahāyopi sahāyena vivadati—
pli-tv-kd14:14.12.10.eng: A mother disputes with her offspring; an offspring with their mother; a father with his offspring; an offspring with their father; a brother with his brother; a brother with his sister; a sister with her brother; a friend with their friend—

pli-tv-kd14:14.12.11.eng: this is a dispute that isn’t also a legal issue.

pli-tv-kd14:14.12.12.eng: How’s there a legal issue that isn’t also a dispute?

pli-tv-kd14:14.12.13.eng: A legal issue arising from an accusation, a legal issue arising from an offense, a legal issue arising from business—

pli-tv-kd14:14.12.14.eng: this is a legal issue that isn’t also a dispute.

pli-tv-kd14:14.12.15.eng: How’s there a legal issue that’s also a dispute?

pli-tv-kd14:14.12.16.eng: A legal issue arising from a dispute is a legal issue and also a dispute.”

pli-tv-kd14:14.13.0.eng: Relationship between accusations and legal issues

pli-tv-kd14:14.13.1.pli: Anuvādo anuvādādhikaraṇaṁ, anuvādo no adhikaraṇaṁ, adhikaraṇaṁ no anuvādo, adhikaraṇañceva anuvādo ca.
pli-tv-kd14:14.13.1.eng: “Are there accusations that are also legal issues arising from accusations? Are there accusations that aren’t also legal issues? Are there legal issues that aren’t also accusations? Are there legal issues that are also accusations?

pli-tv-kd14:14.13.2.pli: Siyā anuvādo anuvādādhikaraṇaṁ, siyā anuvādo no adhikaraṇaṁ, siyā adhikaraṇaṁ no anuvādo, siyā adhikaraṇañceva anuvādo ca.
pli-tv-kd14:14.13.2.eng: There are accusations that are also legal issues arising from accusations. There are accusations that aren’t also legal issues. There are legal issues that aren’t also accusations. There are legal issues that are also accusations.

pli-tv-kd14:14.13.3.eng: How’s there an accusation that’s also a legal issue arising from an accusation?

pli-tv-kd14:14.13.4.pli: Idha pana, bhikkhave, bhikkhū bhikkhuṁ anuvadanti—
pli-tv-kd14:14.13.4.eng: It may be that monks accuse a monk

pli-tv-kd14:14.13.5.pli: sīlavipattiyā vā, ācāravipattiyā vā, diṭṭhivipattiyā vā, ājīvavipattiyā vā.
pli-tv-kd14:14.13.5.eng: of failure in morality, conduct, view, or livelihood.

pli-tv-kd14:14.13.6.pli: Yo tattha anuvādo anuvadanā anullapanā anubhaṇanā anusampavaṅkatā abbhussahanatā anubalappadānaṁ—
pli-tv-kd14:14.13.6.eng: In regard to this, whatever there is of accusations, accusing, allegations, blame, taking sides because of friendship, taking part in the accusation, or supporting the accusation—

pli-tv-kd14:14.13.7.eng: this is an accusation that’s also a legal issue arising from an accusation.

pli-tv-kd14:14.13.8.eng: How’s there an accusation that isn’t also a legal issue?

pli-tv-kd14:14.13.9.pli: Mātāpi puttaṁ anuvadati, puttopi mātaraṁ anuvadati, pitāpi puttaṁ anuvadati, puttopi pitaraṁ anuvadati, bhātāpi bhātaraṁ anuvadati, bhātāpi bhaginiṁ anuvadati, bhaginīpi bhātaraṁ anuvadati, sahāyopi sahāyaṁ anuvadati—
pli-tv-kd14:14.13.9.eng: A mother accuses her offspring; an offspring their mother; a father his offspring; an offspring their father; a brother his brother; a brother his sister; a sister her brother; a friend their friend—

pli-tv-kd14:14.13.10.eng: this is an accusation that isn’t also a legal issue.

pli-tv-kd14:14.13.11.eng: How’s there a legal issue that isn’t also an accusation?

pli-tv-kd14:14.13.12.eng: A legal issue arising from an offense, a legal issue arising from business, a legal issue arising from a dispute—

pli-tv-kd14:14.13.13.eng: this is a legal issue that isn’t also an accusation.

pli-tv-kd14:14.13.14.eng: How’s there a legal issue that’s also an accusation?

pli-tv-kd14:14.13.15.eng: A legal issue arising from an accusation is a legal issue and also an accusation.”

pli-tv-kd14:14.14.0.eng: Relationship between offenses and legal issues

pli-tv-kd14:14.14.1.pli: Āpatti āpattādhikaraṇaṁ, āpatti no adhikaraṇaṁ, adhikaraṇaṁ no āpatti, adhikaraṇañceva āpatti ca.
pli-tv-kd14:14.14.1.eng: “Are there offenses that are also legal issues arising from an offense? Are there offenses that aren’t also legal issues? Are there legal issues that aren’t also offenses? Are there legal issues that are also offenses?

pli-tv-kd14:14.14.2.pli: Siyā āpatti āpattādhikaraṇaṁ, siyā āpatti no adhikaraṇaṁ, siyā adhikaraṇaṁ no āpatti, siyā adhikaraṇañceva āpatti ca.
pli-tv-kd14:14.14.2.eng: There are offenses that are also legal issues arising from an offense. There are offenses that aren’t also legal issues. There are legal issues that aren’t also offenses. There are legal issues that are also offenses.

pli-tv-kd14:14.14.3.eng: How’s there an offense that’s also a legal issue arising from an offense?

pli-tv-kd14:14.14.4.pli: Pañcapi āpattikkhandhā āpattādhikaraṇaṁ, sattapi āpattikkhandhā āpattādhikaraṇaṁ—
pli-tv-kd14:14.14.4.eng: There’s a legal issue arising from an offense because of the five classes of offenses, and there’s a legal issue arising from an offense because of the seven classes of offenses—

pli-tv-kd14:14.14.5.eng: this is an offense that’s also a legal issue arising from an offense.

pli-tv-kd14:14.14.6.eng: How’s there an offense that isn’t also a legal issue?

pli-tv-kd14:14.14.8.eng: this is an attainment/offense that isn’t also a legal issue.
pli-tv-kd14:14.14.8.comm: In the Pali, the word āpatti/samāpatti means both “attainment” and “offense”. The Pali is a play on words, which is impossible to replicate in English.

pli-tv-kd14:14.14.9.eng: How’s there a legal issue that isn’t also an offense?

pli-tv-kd14:14.14.10.eng: A legal issue arising from business, a legal issue arising from a dispute, a legal issue arising from an accusation—

pli-tv-kd14:14.14.11.eng: this is a legal issue that isn’t also an offense.

pli-tv-kd14:14.14.12.eng: How’s there a legal issue that’s also an offense?

pli-tv-kd14:14.14.13.eng: A legal issue arising from an offense is a legal issue and also an offense.”

pli-tv-kd14:14.15.0.eng: Relationship between business and legal issues

pli-tv-kd14:14.15.1.pli: Kiccaṁ kiccādhikaraṇaṁ, kiccaṁ no adhikaraṇaṁ, adhikaraṇaṁ no kiccaṁ, adhikaraṇañceva kiccañca.
pli-tv-kd14:14.15.1.eng: “Is there business that’s also a legal issue arising from business? Is there business that isn’t also a legal issue? Are there legal issues that aren’t also business? Are there legal issues that are also business?

pli-tv-kd14:14.15.2.pli: Siyā kiccaṁ kiccādhikaraṇaṁ, siyā kiccaṁ no adhikaraṇaṁ, siyā adhikaraṇaṁ no kiccaṁ, siyā adhikaraṇañceva kiccañca.
pli-tv-kd14:14.15.2.eng: There’s business that’s also a legal issue arising from business. There’s business that’s not a legal issue. There are legal issues that aren’t also business. There are legal issues that are also business.

pli-tv-kd14:14.15.3.eng: How’s there business that’s also a legal issue arising from business?

pli-tv-kd14:14.15.4.pli: Yā saṅghassa kiccayatā, karaṇīyatā, apalokanakammaṁ, ñattikammaṁ, ñattidutiyakammaṁ, ñatticatutthakammaṁ—
pli-tv-kd14:14.15.4.eng: Whatever is the duty or the business of the Sangha—a legal procedure consisting of getting permission, a legal procedure consisting of one motion, a legal procedure consisting of one motion and one announcement, a legal procedure consisting of one motion and three announcements—

pli-tv-kd14:14.15.5.eng: this is business that’s also a legal issue arising from business.

pli-tv-kd14:14.15.6.eng: How’s there business that isn’t also a legal issue?

pli-tv-kd14:14.15.7.eng: The duty to teacher, the duty to a preceptor, the duty to a co-student, the duty to a co-pupil—

pli-tv-kd14:14.15.8.eng: this is business that isn’t also a legal issue.

pli-tv-kd14:14.15.9.eng: How’s there a legal issue that isn’t also business?

pli-tv-kd14:14.15.10.eng: A legal issue arising from a dispute, a legal issue arising from an accusation, a legal issue arising from an offense—

pli-tv-kd14:14.15.11.eng: this is a legal issue that isn’t also business.

pli-tv-kd14:14.15.12.eng: How’s there a legal issue that’s also business?

pli-tv-kd14:14.15.13.eng: A legal issue arising from business is a legal issue and also business.”

pli-tv-kd14:14.15.14.0.1.eng: 9. The resolution and settling of legal issues

pli-tv-kd14:14.16.1.eng: “There are two principles for settling a legal issue arising from a dispute:

pli-tv-kd14:14.16.3.pli: Siyā vivādādhikaraṇaṁ ekaṁ samathaṁ anāgamma yebhuyyasikaṁ, ekena samathena sameyya—
pli-tv-kd14:14.16.3.eng: Is it possible that a legal issue arising from a dispute should be settled not by majority decision,

pli-tv-kd14:14.16.7.eng: It may be, monks, that monks are disputing, saying,

pli-tv-kd14:14.16.8.pli: dhammoti vā adhammoti vā, vinayoti vā avinayoti vā, bhāsitaṁ lapitaṁ tathāgatenāti vā abhāsitaṁ alapitaṁ tathāgatenāti vā, āciṇṇaṁ tathāgatenāti vā anāciṇṇaṁ tathāgatenāti vā, paññattaṁ tathāgatenāti vā apaññattaṁ tathāgatenāti vā, āpattīti vā anāpattīti vā, lahukā āpattīti vā garukā āpattīti vā, sāvasesā āpattīti vā anavasesā āpattīti vā, duṭṭhullā āpattīti vā aduṭṭhullā āpattīti vā.
pli-tv-kd14:14.16.8.eng: ‘This is the Teaching’, ‘This is contrary to the Teaching’, ‘This is the Monastic Law’, ‘This is contrary to the Monastic Law’, ‘This was spoken by the Buddha’, ‘This wasn’t spoken by the Buddha’, ‘This was practiced by the Buddha’, ‘This wasn’t practiced by the Buddha’, ‘This was laid down by the Buddha’, ‘This wasn’t laid down by the Buddha’, ‘This is an offense’, ‘This isn’t an offense’, ‘This is a light offense’, ‘This is a heavy offense’, ‘This is a curable offense’, ‘This is an incurable offense’, ‘This is a grave offense’, or ‘This is a minor offense.’

pli-tv-kd14:14.16.9.pli: Te ce, bhikkhave, bhikkhū sakkonti taṁ adhikaraṇaṁ vūpasametuṁ, idaṁ vuccati, bhikkhave, adhikaraṇaṁ vūpasantaṁ.
pli-tv-kd14:14.16.9.eng: If those monks are able to resolve that legal issue, this is called the resolution of a legal issue.

pli-tv-kd14:14.16.13.eng: Face-to-face with the Sangha, the Teaching, the Monastic Law, and the persons concerned.

pli-tv-kd14:14.16.14.eng: This is the meaning of face-to-face with the Sangha:

pli-tv-kd14:14.16.15.pli: Yāvatikā bhikkhū kammappattā te āgatā honti, chandārahānaṁ chando āhaṭo hoti, sammukhībhūtā na paṭikkosanti—
pli-tv-kd14:14.16.15.eng: the monks who should be present have arrived, consent has been brought for those who are eligible to give their consent, and no-one present objects to the decision.

pli-tv-kd14:14.16.17.eng: This is the meaning of face-to-face with the Teaching and the Monastic Law:

pli-tv-kd14:14.16.18.pli: Yena dhammena yena vinayena yena satthusāsanena taṁ adhikaraṇaṁ vūpasammati—
pli-tv-kd14:14.16.18.eng: the Teaching, the Monastic Law, the Teacher’s instruction—that by which that legal issue is resolved.

pli-tv-kd14:14.16.20.eng: This is the meaning of face-to-face with the persons concerned:

pli-tv-kd14:14.16.21.pli: Yo ca vivadati, yena ca vivadati, ubho atthapaccatthikā sammukhībhūtā honti—
pli-tv-kd14:14.16.21.eng: both sides—those who are disputing and those they’re disputing with—are present.

pli-tv-kd14:14.16.23.pli: Evaṁ vūpasantañce, bhikkhave, adhikaraṇaṁ kārako ukkoṭeti, ukkoṭanakaṁ pācittiyaṁ;
pli-tv-kd14:14.16.23.eng: When a legal issue has been resolved like this, if any of the participants reopen it, they incur an offense entailing confession for the reopening.

pli-tv-kd14:14.16.24.eng: If anyone who gave their consent criticizes the resolution, they incur an offense entailing confession.

pli-tv-kd14:14.17.1.pli: Te ce, bhikkhave, bhikkhū na sakkonti taṁ adhikaraṇaṁ tasmiṁ āvāse vūpasametuṁ, tehi, bhikkhave, bhikkhūhi, yasmiṁ āvāse sambahulā bhikkhū, so āvāso gantabbo.
pli-tv-kd14:14.17.1.eng: If those monks are unable to resolve that legal issue in that monastery, they should go to another monastery that has a number of monks.

pli-tv-kd14:14.17.2.pli: Te ce, bhikkhave, bhikkhū taṁ āvāsaṁ gacchantā antarāmagge sakkonti taṁ adhikaraṇaṁ vūpasametuṁ, idaṁ vuccati, bhikkhave, adhikaraṇaṁ vūpasantaṁ.
pli-tv-kd14:14.17.2.eng: If they’re able to resolve that legal issue while on their way, this is called the resolution of a legal issue.

pli-tv-kd14:14.17.6.eng: Face-to-face with the Sangha, the Teaching, the Monastic Law, and the persons concerned.

pli-tv-kd14:14.17.8.pli: Yāvatikā bhikkhū kammappattā te āgatā honti, chandārahānaṁ chando āhaṭo hoti, sammukhībhūtā na paṭikkosanti—

pli-tv-kd14:14.17.11.pli: Yena dhammena yena vinayena yena satthusāsanena taṁ adhikaraṇaṁ vūpasammati—

pli-tv-kd14:14.17.14.pli: Yo ca vivadati, yena ca vivadati, ubho atthapaccatthikā sammukhībhūtā honti—

pli-tv-kd14:14.17.16.pli: Evaṁ vūpasantañce, bhikkhave, adhikaraṇaṁ kārako ukkoṭeti, ukkoṭanakaṁ pācittiyaṁ;
pli-tv-kd14:14.17.16.eng: When a legal issue has been resolved like this, if any of the participants reopen it, they incur an offense entailing confession for the reopening.

pli-tv-kd14:14.17.17.eng: If anyone who gave their consent criticizes the resolution, they incur an offense entailing confession.

pli-tv-kd14:14.18.1.pli: Te ce, bhikkhave, bhikkhū taṁ āvāsaṁ āgacchantā antarāmagge na sakkonti taṁ adhikaraṇaṁ vūpasametuṁ, tehi, bhikkhave, bhikkhūhi, taṁ āvāsaṁ gantvā āvāsikā bhikkhū evamassu vacanīyā—
pli-tv-kd14:14.18.1.eng: If those monks are unable to resolve that legal issue while on their way, they should go to that other monastery and say to the resident monks,

pli-tv-kd14:14.18.2.pli: ‘idaṁ kho, āvuso, adhikaraṇaṁ evaṁ jātaṁ, evaṁ samuppannaṁ;
pli-tv-kd14:14.18.2.eng: ‘This legal issue has come about in such-and-such a way.

pli-tv-kd14:14.18.3.pli: sādhāyasmantā imaṁ adhikaraṇaṁ vūpasamentu dhammena vinayena satthusāsanena, yathayidaṁ adhikaraṇaṁ suvūpasantaṁ assā’ti.
pli-tv-kd14:14.18.3.eng: Please resolve it, venerables, according to the Teaching, the Monastic Law, and the Teacher’s instruction, so that this legal issue may be properly disposed of.’

pli-tv-kd14:14.18.4.pli: Sace, bhikkhave, āvāsikā bhikkhū vuḍḍhatarā honti, āgantukā bhikkhū navakatarā, tehi, bhikkhave, āvāsikehi bhikkhūhi āgantukā bhikkhū evamassu vacanīyā—
pli-tv-kd14:14.18.4.eng: If the resident monks are senior to the newly-arrived monks, the resident monks should say,

pli-tv-kd14:14.18.5.pli: ‘iṅgha tumhe, āyasmanto, muhuttaṁ ekamantaṁ hotha, yāva mayaṁ mantemā’ti.
pli-tv-kd14:14.18.5.eng: ‘Now, venerables, please go to one side for a moment while we discuss this matter.’

pli-tv-kd14:14.18.6.pli: Sace pana, bhikkhave, āvāsikā bhikkhū navakatarā honti, āgantukā bhikkhū vuḍḍhatarā, tehi, bhikkhave, āvāsikehi bhikkhūhi āgantukā bhikkhū evamassu vacanīyā—
pli-tv-kd14:14.18.6.eng: If the newly-arrived monks are senior to the resident monks, the resident monks should say,

pli-tv-kd14:14.18.7.pli: ‘tena hi tumhe, āyasmanto, muhuttaṁ idheva tāva hotha, yāva mayaṁ mantemā’ti.
pli-tv-kd14:14.18.7.eng: ‘Well then, venerables, please wait right here for a moment while we discuss this matter.’

pli-tv-kd14:14.18.8.pli: Sace pana, bhikkhave, āvāsikānaṁ bhikkhūnaṁ mantayamānānaṁ evaṁ hoti—
pli-tv-kd14:14.18.8.eng: If, while discussing that matter, the resident monks think,

pli-tv-kd14:14.18.9.pli: ‘na mayaṁ sakkoma imaṁ adhikaraṇaṁ vūpasametuṁ dhammena vinayena satthusāsanenā’ti, na taṁ adhikaraṇaṁ āvāsikehi bhikkhūhi sampaṭicchitabbaṁ.
pli-tv-kd14:14.18.9.eng: ‘We’re unable to resolve this legal issue according to the Teaching, the Monastic Law, and the Teacher’s instruction,’ then they shouldn’t take on that legal issue.

pli-tv-kd14:14.18.10.pli: Sace pana, bhikkhave, āvāsikānaṁ bhikkhūnaṁ mantayamānānaṁ evaṁ hoti—

pli-tv-kd14:14.18.11.pli: ‘sakkoma mayaṁ imaṁ adhikaraṇaṁ vūpasametuṁ dhammena vinayena satthusāsanenā’ti.
pli-tv-kd14:14.18.11.eng: ‘We’re able to resolve this legal issue according to the Teaching, the Monastic Law, and the Teacher’s instruction,’

pli-tv-kd14:14.18.12.pli: Tehi, bhikkhave, āvāsikehi bhikkhūhi āgantukā bhikkhū evamassu vacanīyā—
pli-tv-kd14:14.18.12.eng: then they should say to the newly-arrived monks,

pli-tv-kd14:14.18.13.pli: ‘sace tumhe, āyasmanto, amhākaṁ imaṁ adhikaraṇaṁ yathājātaṁ yathāsamuppannaṁ ārocessatha, yathā ca mayaṁ imaṁ adhikaraṇaṁ vūpasamessāma dhammena vinayena satthusāsanena tathā suvūpasantaṁ bhavissati.
pli-tv-kd14:14.18.13.eng: ‘If you’ll tell us how this legal issue came about, we’ll be able to dispose of it properly according to the Teaching, the Monastic Law, and the Teacher’s instruction.

pli-tv-kd14:14.18.14.eng: And so we’ll take it on.

pli-tv-kd14:14.18.15.pli: No ce tumhe, āyasmanto, amhākaṁ imaṁ adhikaraṇaṁ yathājātaṁ yathāsamuppannaṁ ārocessatha, yathā ca mayaṁ imaṁ adhikaraṇaṁ vūpasamessāma dhammena vinayena satthusāsanena tathā na suvūpasantaṁ bhavissati, na mayaṁ imaṁ adhikaraṇaṁ sampaṭicchissāmā’ti.
pli-tv-kd14:14.18.15.eng: If, however, you won’t tell us, we won’t be able to properly dispose of it. And so we won’t take it on.’

pli-tv-kd14:14.18.16.pli: Evaṁ supariggahitaṁ kho, bhikkhave, katvā āvāsikehi bhikkhūhi taṁ adhikaraṇaṁ sampaṭicchitabbaṁ.
pli-tv-kd14:14.18.16.eng: Having properly examined it like this, the resident monks should take it on.

pli-tv-kd14:14.18.17.pli: Tehi, bhikkhave, āgantukehi bhikkhūhi āvāsikā bhikkhū evamassu vacanīyā—
pli-tv-kd14:14.18.17.eng: The newly-arrived monks should say this to the resident monks,

pli-tv-kd14:14.18.18.pli: ‘yathājātaṁ yathāsamuppannaṁ mayaṁ imaṁ adhikaraṇaṁ āyasmantānaṁ ārocessāma.
pli-tv-kd14:14.18.18.eng: ‘We’ll tell you how this legal issue came about.

pli-tv-kd14:14.18.19.pli: Sace āyasmantā sakkonti ettakena vā ettakena vā antarena imaṁ adhikaraṇaṁ vūpasametuṁ dhammena vinayena satthusāsanena tathā suvūpasantaṁ bhavissati.
pli-tv-kd14:14.18.19.eng: If you’re able to dispose of it properly by such and such means—according to the Teaching, the Monastic Law, and the Teacher’s instruction—
pli-tv-kd14:14.18.19.comm: Sp-ṭ 4.230: Antarenāti kāraṇena, “Antarena: by means of”.

pli-tv-kd14:14.18.20.pli: Evaṁ mayaṁ imaṁ adhikaraṇaṁ āyasmantānaṁ niyyādessāma.
pli-tv-kd14:14.18.20.eng: then we’ll hand it over to you.

pli-tv-kd14:14.18.21.pli: No ce āyasmantā sakkonti ettakena vā ettakena vā antarena imaṁ adhikaraṇaṁ vūpasametuṁ dhammena vinayena satthusāsanena tathā na suvūpasantaṁ bhavissati, na mayaṁ imaṁ adhikaraṇaṁ āyasmantānaṁ niyyādessāma.
pli-tv-kd14:14.18.21.eng: If you’re unable to properly dispose of it  by such and such means, then we won’t hand it over to you.

pli-tv-kd14:14.18.22.eng: We’ll take responsibility for it ourselves.’

pli-tv-kd14:14.18.23.pli: Evaṁ supariggahitaṁ kho, bhikkhave, katvā āgantukehi bhikkhūhi taṁ adhikaraṇaṁ āvāsikānaṁ bhikkhūnaṁ niyyādetabbaṁ.
pli-tv-kd14:14.18.23.eng: Having properly examined it like this, the newly-arrived monks should hand it over to the resident monks.

pli-tv-kd14:14.18.24.pli: Te ce, bhikkhave, bhikkhū sakkonti taṁ adhikaraṇaṁ vūpasametuṁ, idaṁ vuccati, bhikkhave, adhikaraṇaṁ vūpasantaṁ.
pli-tv-kd14:14.18.24.eng: If those monks are able to resolve that legal issue, this is called the resolution of a legal issue.

pli-tv-kd14:14.18.28.eng: Face-to-face with the Sangha, the Teaching, the Monastic Law, and the persons concerned. …

pli-tv-kd14:14.18.29.pli: evaṁ vūpasantañce, bhikkhave, adhikaraṇaṁ kārako ukkoṭeti, ukkoṭanakaṁ pācittiyaṁ;
pli-tv-kd14:14.18.29.eng: When a legal issue has been resolved like this, if any of the participants reopen it, they incur an offense entailing confession for the reopening.

pli-tv-kd14:14.18.30.eng: If anyone who gave their consent criticizes the resolution, they incur an offense entailing confession.”

pli-tv-kd14:14.19.1.pli: Tehi ce, bhikkhave, bhikkhūhi tasmiṁ adhikaraṇe vinicchiyamāne anantāni ceva bhassāni jāyanti, na cekassa bhāsitassa attho viññāyati, anujānāmi, bhikkhave, evarūpaṁ adhikaraṇaṁ ubbāhikāya vūpasametuṁ.
pli-tv-kd14:14.19.1.eng: “If, monks, while they’re discussing that legal issue, there’s endless talk, but not a single statement that’s clear, then they should resolve that legal issue by means of a committee.

pli-tv-kd14:14.19.2.eng: A monk who has ten qualities may be appointed to that committee:

pli-tv-kd14:14.19.3.pli: sīlavā hoti, pātimokkhasaṁvarasaṁvuto viharati ācāragocarasampanno aṇumattesu vajjesu bhayadassāvī, samādāya sikkhati sikkhāpadesu;
pli-tv-kd14:14.19.3.eng: One who’s virtuous and restrained by the Monastic Code. His conduct is good, he associates with the right people, and he sees danger in minor faults. He undertakes and trains in the training rules.

pli-tv-kd14:14.19.4.pli: bahussuto hoti sutadharo sutasannicayo, ye te dhammā ādikalyāṇā majjhekalyāṇā pariyosānakalyāṇā sātthaṁ sabyañjanaṁ kevalaparipuṇṇaṁ parisuddhaṁ brahmacariyaṁ abhivadanti, tathārūpassa dhammā bahussutā honti, dhātā vacasā paricitā manasānupekkhitā diṭṭhiyā suppaṭividdhā;
pli-tv-kd14:14.19.4.eng: One who has learned much, and who retains and accumulates what he has learned. Those teachings that are good in the beginning, good in the middle, and good in the end, that have a true goal and are well articulated, and that set out the perfectly complete and pure spiritual life—he has learned many such teachings, retained them in mind, recited them verbally, mentally investigated them, and penetrated them well by view.

pli-tv-kd14:14.19.5.pli: ubhayāni kho panassa pātimokkhāni vitthārena svāgatāni honti suvibhattāni suppavattīni suvinicchitāni suttaso anubyañjanaso;
pli-tv-kd14:14.19.5.eng: One who has properly learned both Monastic Codes in detail; who has analyzed them well, thoroughly mastered them, and investigated them well, both in terms of the rules and their detailed exposition.

pli-tv-kd14:14.19.6.pli: vinaye kho pana ṭhito hoti asaṁhīro;
pli-tv-kd14:14.19.6.eng: One who is firmly committed to the Monastic Law.

pli-tv-kd14:14.19.7.pli: paṭibalo hoti ubho atthapaccatthike assāsetuṁ saññāpetuṁ nijjhāpetuṁ pekkhetuṁ pasādetuṁ;
pli-tv-kd14:14.19.7.eng: One who is capable of making both sides relax, of persuading them, of convincing them, of making them see, of reconciling them.

pli-tv-kd14:14.19.8.eng: One who is knowledgeable about the arising and resolution of legal issues.

pli-tv-kd14:14.19.10.eng: One who understands the arising of legal issues.

pli-tv-kd14:14.19.11.eng: One who understands the ending of legal issues.

pli-tv-kd14:14.19.12.eng: One who understands the way to the ending of legal issues.

pli-tv-kd14:14.19.13.pli: Anujānāmi, bhikkhave, imehi dasahaṅgehi samannāgataṁ bhikkhuṁ ubbāhikāya sammannituṁ.

pli-tv-kd14:14.20.1.eng: And he should be appointed like this.

pli-tv-kd14:14.20.2.pli: Paṭhamaṁ bhikkhu yācitabbo, yācitvā byattena bhikkhunā paṭibalena saṅgho ñāpetabbo—
pli-tv-kd14:14.20.2.eng: First a monk should be asked, and then a competent and capable monk should inform the Sangha:

pli-tv-kd14:14.20.3.eng: ‘Please, venerables, I ask the Sangha to listen.

pli-tv-kd14:14.20.4.pli: Amhākaṁ imasmiṁ adhikaraṇe vinicchiyamāne anantāni ceva bhassāni jāyanti, na cekassa bhāsitassa attho viññāyati.
pli-tv-kd14:14.20.4.eng: While we were discussing this legal issue, there was endless talk, but not a single statement that was clear.

pli-tv-kd14:14.20.5.pli: Yadi saṅghassa pattakallaṁ, saṅgho itthannāmañca itthannāmañca bhikkhuṁ sammanneyya ubbāhikāya imaṁ adhikaraṇaṁ vūpasametuṁ.
pli-tv-kd14:14.20.5.eng: If the Sangha is ready, it should appoint monk so-and-so and monk so-and-so to a committee to resolve this legal issue.

pli-tv-kd14:14.20.7.eng: Please, venerables, I ask the Sangha to listen.

pli-tv-kd14:14.20.8.pli: Amhākaṁ imasmiṁ adhikaraṇe vinicchiyamāne anantāni ceva bhassāni jāyanti, na cekassa bhāsitassa attho viññāyati.
pli-tv-kd14:14.20.8.eng: While we were discussing this legal issue, there was endless talk, but not a single statement that was clear.

pli-tv-kd14:14.20.9.pli: Saṅgho itthannāmañca itthannāmañca bhikkhuṁ sammannati ubbāhikāya imaṁ adhikaraṇaṁ vūpasametuṁ.
pli-tv-kd14:14.20.9.eng: The Sangha appoints monk so-and-so and monk so-and-so to a committee to resolve this legal issue.

pli-tv-kd14:14.20.10.pli: Yassāyasmato khamati itthannāmassa ca itthannāmassa ca bhikkhuno sammuti ubbāhikāya imaṁ adhikaraṇaṁ vūpasametuṁ, so tuṇhassa;
pli-tv-kd14:14.20.10.eng: Any monk who approves of appointing monk so-and-so and monk so-and-so to a committee should remain silent.

pli-tv-kd14:14.20.11.eng: Any monk who doesn’t approve should speak up.

pli-tv-kd14:14.20.12.pli: Sammato saṅghena itthannāmo ca itthannāmo ca bhikkhu ubbāhikāya imaṁ adhikaraṇaṁ vūpasametuṁ.
pli-tv-kd14:14.20.12.eng: The Sangha has appointed monk so-and-so and monk so-and-so to a committee to resolve this legal issue.

pli-tv-kd14:14.20.13.pli: Khamati saṅghassa, tasmā tuṇhī, evametaṁ dhārayāmī’ti.
pli-tv-kd14:14.20.13.eng: The Sangha approves and is therefore silent. I’ll remember it thus.’

pli-tv-kd14:14.21.1.pli: Te ce, bhikkhave, bhikkhū sakkonti taṁ adhikaraṇaṁ ubbāhikāya vūpasametuṁ, idaṁ vuccati, bhikkhave, adhikaraṇaṁ vūpasantaṁ.
pli-tv-kd14:14.21.1.eng: If those monks are able to resolve that legal issue by committee, this is called the resolution of a legal issue.

pli-tv-kd14:14.21.5.eng: Face-to-face with the Sangha, the Teaching, the Monastic Law, and the persons concerned. This is the meaning of face-to-face with the Sangha: the monks who should be present have arrived, consent has been brought for those who are eligible to give their consent, and no-one present objects to the decision. This is the meaning of face-to-face with the Teaching and the Monastic Law: the Teaching, the Monastic Law, the Teacher’s instruction—that by which that legal issue is resolved. This is the meaning of face-to-face with the persons concerned: both sides—those who are disputing and those they’re disputing with—are present.

pli-tv-kd14:14.21.6.pli: evaṁ vūpasantañce, bhikkhave, adhikaraṇaṁ kārako ukkoṭeti, ukkoṭanakaṁ pācittiyaṁ.
pli-tv-kd14:14.21.6.eng: When a legal issue has been resolved like this, if any of the participants reopen it, they incur an offense entailing confession for the reopening.

pli-tv-kd14:14.22.1.pli: Tehi ce, bhikkhave, bhikkhūhi tasmiṁ adhikaraṇe vinicchiyamāne tatrāssa bhikkhu dhammakathiko, tassa neva suttaṁ āgataṁ hoti, no suttavibhaṅgo, so atthaṁ asallakkhento byañjanacchāyāya atthaṁ paṭibāhati, byattena bhikkhunā paṭibalena te bhikkhū ñāpetabbā—
pli-tv-kd14:14.22.1.eng: While those monks are discussing that legal issue, there may be a monk there who’s an expounder of the Teaching, but who doesn’t know the Monastic Code or its analysis. Not understanding the meaning, he obstructs it by obscuring it with the wording. A competent and capable monk should then inform the Sangha:

pli-tv-kd14:14.22.2.eng: ‘Please, venerables, I ask you to listen.

pli-tv-kd14:14.22.3.eng: The monk so-and-so is an expounder of the Teaching,

pli-tv-kd14:14.22.4.pli: Imassa neva suttaṁ āgataṁ hoti, no suttavibhaṅgo.
pli-tv-kd14:14.22.4.eng: but doesn’t know the Monastic Code or its analysis.

pli-tv-kd14:14.22.5.pli: So atthaṁ asallakkhento byañjanacchāyāya atthaṁ paṭibāhati.
pli-tv-kd14:14.22.5.eng: Not understanding the meaning, he obstructs it by obscuring it with the wording.

pli-tv-kd14:14.22.6.pli: Yadāyasmantānaṁ pattakallaṁ, itthannāmaṁ bhikkhuṁ vuṭṭhāpetvā avasesā imaṁ adhikaraṇaṁ vūpasameyyāmā’ti.
pli-tv-kd14:14.22.6.eng: If the venerables are ready, we should ask monk so-and-so to leave, with the remainder of us resolving this legal issue.’

pli-tv-kd14:14.22.7.pli: Te ce, bhikkhave, bhikkhū taṁ bhikkhuṁ vuṭṭhāpetvā sakkonti taṁ adhikaraṇaṁ vūpasametuṁ, idaṁ vuccati, bhikkhave, adhikaraṇaṁ vūpasantaṁ.
pli-tv-kd14:14.22.7.eng: If, after that monk has left, those monks are able to resolve that legal issue, this is called the resolution of a legal issue.

pli-tv-kd14:14.22.11.eng: Face-to-face with the Sangha, the Teaching, the Monastic Law, and the persons concerned. …

pli-tv-kd14:14.22.12.pli: evaṁ vūpasantañce, bhikkhave, adhikaraṇaṁ kārako ukkoṭeti, ukkoṭanakaṁ pācittiyaṁ.
pli-tv-kd14:14.22.12.eng: When a legal issue has been resolved like this, if any of the participants reopen it, they incur an offense entailing confession for the reopening.

pli-tv-kd14:14.23.1.pli: Tehi ce, bhikkhave, bhikkhūhi tasmiṁ adhikaraṇe vinicchiyamāne tatrāssa bhikkhu dhammakathiko, tassa suttañhi kho āgataṁ hoti, no suttavibhaṅgo, so atthaṁ asallakkhento byañjanacchāyāya atthaṁ paṭibāhati, byattena bhikkhunā paṭibalena te bhikkhū ñāpetabbā—
pli-tv-kd14:14.23.1.eng: While those monks are discussing that legal issue, there may be a monk there who’s an expounder of the Teaching. He knows the Monastic Code, but not its analysis. Not understanding the meaning, he obstructs it by obscuring it with the wording. A competent and capable monk should then inform the Sangha:

pli-tv-kd14:14.23.2.eng: ‘Please, venerables, I ask you to listen.

pli-tv-kd14:14.23.3.eng: The monk so-and-so is an expounder of the Teaching.

pli-tv-kd14:14.23.4.pli: Imassa suttañhi kho āgataṁ hoti, no suttavibhaṅgo.
pli-tv-kd14:14.23.4.eng: He knows the Monastic Code, but not its analysis.

pli-tv-kd14:14.23.5.pli: So atthaṁ asallakkhento byañjanacchāyāya atthaṁ paṭibāhati.
pli-tv-kd14:14.23.5.eng: Not understanding the meaning, he obstructs it by obscuring it with the wording.

pli-tv-kd14:14.23.6.pli: Yadāyasmantānaṁ pattakallaṁ, itthannāmaṁ bhikkhuṁ vuṭṭhāpetvā avasesā imaṁ adhikaraṇaṁ vūpasameyyāmā’ti.
pli-tv-kd14:14.23.6.eng: If the venerables are ready, we should ask monk so-and-so to leave, with the remainder of us resolving this legal issue.’

pli-tv-kd14:14.23.7.pli: Te ce, bhikkhave, bhikkhū taṁ bhikkhuṁ vuṭṭhāpetvā sakkonti taṁ adhikaraṇaṁ vūpasametuṁ, idaṁ vuccati, bhikkhave, adhikaraṇaṁ vūpasantaṁ.
pli-tv-kd14:14.23.7.eng: If, after that monk has left, those monks are able to resolve that legal issue, this is called the resolution of a legal issue.

pli-tv-kd14:14.23.11.eng: Face-to-face with the Sangha, the Teaching, the Monastic Law, and the persons concerned. …

pli-tv-kd14:14.23.12.pli: evaṁ vūpasantañce, bhikkhave, adhikaraṇaṁ kārako ukkoṭeti, ukkoṭanakaṁ pācittiyaṁ.
pli-tv-kd14:14.23.12.eng: When a legal issue has been resolved like this, if any of the participants reopen it, they incur an offense entailing confession for the reopening.”

pli-tv-kd14:14.24.1.pli: Te ce, bhikkhave, bhikkhū na sakkonti taṁ adhikaraṇaṁ ubbāhikāya vūpasametuṁ, tehi, bhikkhave, bhikkhūhi taṁ adhikaraṇaṁ saṅghassa niyyādetabbaṁ—
pli-tv-kd14:14.24.1.eng: “If, monks, those monks are unable to resolve that legal issue by committee, they should hand it over to the Sangha, saying,

pli-tv-kd14:14.24.2.pli: ‘na mayaṁ, bhante, sakkoma imaṁ adhikaraṇaṁ ubbāhikāya vūpasametuṁ, saṅghova imaṁ adhikaraṇaṁ vūpasametū’ti.
pli-tv-kd14:14.24.2.eng: ‘Venerables, we’re unable to resolve this legal issue by committee. Can the Sangha please resolve it.’

pli-tv-kd14:14.24.3.pli: Anujānāmi, bhikkhave, evarūpaṁ adhikaraṇaṁ yebhuyyasikāya vūpasametuṁ.
pli-tv-kd14:14.24.3.eng: I allow such a legal issue to be resolved by majority decision.

pli-tv-kd14:14.24.4.eng: A monk who has five qualities should be appointed as the manager of the vote:

pli-tv-kd14:14.24.5.pli: yo na chandāgatiṁ gaccheyya, na dosāgatiṁ gaccheyya, na mohāgatiṁ gaccheyya, na bhayāgatiṁ gaccheyya, gahitāgahitañca jāneyya …pe…
pli-tv-kd14:14.24.5.eng: one who isn’t biased by favoritism, ill will, confusion, or fear, and who knows who has and who hasn’t voted.
pli-tv-kd14:14.24.5.comm: “Who knows who has and who hasn’t voted” renders gahitāgahitañca jāneyya, literally, “He should know taken and not taken.” The taking refers to the taking of ballots, salāka.

pli-tv-kd14:14.24.6.eng: And he should be appointed like this.

pli-tv-kd14:14.24.7.pli: Paṭhamaṁ bhikkhu yācitabbo, yācitvā byattena bhikkhunā paṭibalena saṅgho ñāpetabbo—
pli-tv-kd14:14.24.7.eng: First a monk should be asked, and then a competent and capable monk should inform the Sangha:

pli-tv-kd14:14.24.8.eng: ‘Please, venerables, I ask the Sangha to listen.

pli-tv-kd14:14.24.9.pli: Yadi saṅghassa pattakallaṁ, saṅgho itthannāmaṁ bhikkhuṁ salākaggāhāpakaṁ sammanneyya.
pli-tv-kd14:14.24.9.eng: If the Sangha is ready, it should appoint monk so-and-so as the manager of the vote.

pli-tv-kd14:14.24.11.eng: Please, venerables, I ask the Sangha to listen.

pli-tv-kd14:14.24.12.eng: The Sangha appoints monk so-and-so as the manager of the vote.

pli-tv-kd14:14.24.13.pli: Yassāyasmato khamati itthannāmassa bhikkhuno salākaggāhāpakassa sammuti, so tuṇhassa;
pli-tv-kd14:14.24.13.eng: Any monk who approves of appointing monk so-and-so as the manager of the vote should remain silent.

pli-tv-kd14:14.24.14.eng: Any monk who doesn’t approve should speak up.

pli-tv-kd14:14.24.15.eng: The Sangha has appointed monk so-and-so as the manager of the vote.

pli-tv-kd14:14.24.16.pli: Khamati saṅghassa, tasmā tuṇhī, evametaṁ dhārayāmī’ti.
pli-tv-kd14:14.24.16.eng: The Sangha approves and is therefore silent. I’ll remember it thus.’

pli-tv-kd14:14.24.17.eng: That monk should then distribute the ballots.

pli-tv-kd14:14.24.18.pli: Yathā bahutarā bhikkhū dhammavādino vadanti tathā taṁ adhikaraṇaṁ vūpasametabbaṁ.
pli-tv-kd14:14.24.18.eng: If the majority of monks vote in accordance with the Teaching, then that legal issue has been resolved.

pli-tv-kd14:14.24.19.eng: This is called the resolution of a legal issue.

pli-tv-kd14:14.24.21.eng: It’s been resolved face-to-face and by majority decision.

pli-tv-kd14:14.24.23.eng: Face-to-face with the Sangha, the Teaching, the Monastic Law, and the persons concerned.

pli-tv-kd14:14.24.24.eng: This is the meaning of face-to-face with the Sangha:

pli-tv-kd14:14.24.25.pli: Yāvatikā bhikkhū kammappattā te āgatā honti, chandārahānaṁ chando āhaṭo hoti, sammukhībhūtā na paṭikkosanti—
pli-tv-kd14:14.24.25.eng: the monks who should be present have arrived, consent has been brought for those who are eligible to give their consent, and no-one present objects to the decision.

pli-tv-kd14:14.24.27.eng: This is the meaning of face-to-face with the Teaching and the Monastic Law:

pli-tv-kd14:14.24.28.pli: Yena dhammena yena vinayena yena satthusāsanena taṁ adhikaraṇaṁ vūpasammati—
pli-tv-kd14:14.24.28.eng: the Teaching, the Monastic Law, the Teacher’s instruction—that by which that legal issue is resolved.

pli-tv-kd14:14.24.30.eng: This is the meaning of face-to-face with the persons concerned:

pli-tv-kd14:14.24.31.pli: Yo ca vivadati, yena ca vivadati, ubho atthapaccatthikā sammukhībhūtā honti—
pli-tv-kd14:14.24.31.eng: both sides—those who are disputing and those they’re disputing with—are present.

pli-tv-kd14:14.24.33.eng: This is the meaning of by majority decision:

pli-tv-kd14:14.24.34.pli: Yā yebhuyyasikākammassa kiriyā karaṇaṁ upagamanaṁ ajjhupagamanaṁ adhivāsanā appaṭikkosanā—
pli-tv-kd14:14.24.34.eng: the doing of, the performing of, the participation in, the consent to, the agreement to, the non-objection to that legal procedure of majority decision.

pli-tv-kd14:14.24.36.pli: Evaṁ vūpasantañce, bhikkhave, adhikaraṇaṁ kārako ukkoṭeti, ukkoṭanakaṁ pācittiyaṁ;
pli-tv-kd14:14.24.36.eng: When a legal issue has been resolved like this, if any of the participants reopen it, they incur an offense entailing confession for the reopening.

pli-tv-kd14:14.24.37.eng: If anyone who gave their consent criticizes the resolution, they incur an offense entailing confession.”

pli-tv-kd14:14.25.1.pli: Tena kho pana samayena sāvatthiyā evaṁ jātaṁ evaṁ samuppannaṁ adhikaraṇaṁ hoti.
pli-tv-kd14:14.25.1.eng: At one time at Sāvatthī a legal issue had come about in this way.
pli-tv-kd14:14.25.1.comm: That is, it had come about in accordance with the immediately preceding discussion.

pli-tv-kd14:14.25.3.eng: who were dissatisfied with how the Sangha at Sāvatthī had resolved it.

pli-tv-kd14:14.25.4.pli: assosuṁ kho amukasmiṁ kira āvāse sambahulā therā viharanti bahussutā āgatāgamā dhammadharā vinayadharā mātikādharā paṇḍitā viyattā medhāvino lajjino kukkuccakā sikkhākāmā.
pli-tv-kd14:14.25.4.eng: They heard that in a certain monastery there was a number of senior monks who were learned and masters of the tradition; who were experts on the Teaching, the Monastic Law, and the Key Terms; who were knowledgeable and competent, had a sense of conscience, and were afraid of wrongdoing and fond of the training.

pli-tv-kd14:14.25.5.pli: Te ce therā imaṁ adhikaraṇaṁ vūpasameyyuṁ dhammena vinayena satthusāsanena, evamidaṁ adhikaraṇaṁ suvūpasantaṁ assāti.
pli-tv-kd14:14.25.5.eng: They thought, “If these senior monks resolve this legal issue—according to the Teaching, the Monastic Law, and the Teacher’s instruction—then it will be properly disposed of.”

pli-tv-kd14:14.25.6.pli: Atha kho te bhikkhū taṁ āvāsaṁ gantvā te there etadavocuṁ—
pli-tv-kd14:14.25.6.eng: They then went to that monastery and said to those senior monks,

pli-tv-kd14:14.25.7.pli: “idaṁ, bhante, adhikaraṇaṁ evaṁ jātaṁ, evaṁ samuppannaṁ.
pli-tv-kd14:14.25.7.eng: “This legal issue has come about like this.

pli-tv-kd14:14.25.8.pli: Sādhu, bhante, therā imaṁ adhikaraṇaṁ vūpasamentu dhammena vinayena satthusāsanena, yathayidaṁ adhikaraṇaṁ suvūpasantaṁ assā”ti.
pli-tv-kd14:14.25.8.eng: Venerables, please resolve it—according to the Teaching, the Monastic Law, and the Teacher’s instruction—so that it’ll be properly disposed of.”

pli-tv-kd14:14.25.10.pli: yathā sāvatthiyā saṅghena adhikaraṇaṁ vūpasamitaṁ tathā suvūpasantanti—
pli-tv-kd14:14.25.10.eng: “This legal issue was properly disposed of by the Sangha at Sāvatthī,”

pli-tv-kd14:14.25.11.eng: and they resolved it in the same way.

pli-tv-kd14:14.25.13.pli: asantuṭṭhā sāvatthiyā saṅghassa adhikaraṇavūpasamanena, asantuṭṭhā sambahulānaṁ therānaṁ adhikaraṇavūpasamanena—
pli-tv-kd14:14.25.13.eng: were dissatisfied with how the Sangha at Sāvatthī had resolved that legal issue and also with how that number of senior monks had resolved it.

pli-tv-kd14:14.25.14.pli: assosuṁ kho amukasmiṁ kira āvāse tayo therā viharanti …pe…
pli-tv-kd14:14.25.14.eng: They then heard that in a certain monastery there were three senior monks …

pli-tv-kd14:14.25.16.pli: eko thero viharati bahussuto āgatāgamo dhammadharo vinayadharo mātikādharo paṇḍito viyatto medhāvī lajjī kukkuccako sikkhākāmo.
pli-tv-kd14:14.25.16.eng: one senior monk who was learned and a master of the tradition; who was an expert on the Teaching, the Monastic Law, and the Key Terms; who was knowledgeable and competent, had a sense of conscience, and was afraid of wrongdoing and fond of the training.

pli-tv-kd14:14.25.17.pli: So ce thero imaṁ adhikaraṇaṁ vūpasameyya dhammena vinayena satthusāsanena, evamidaṁ adhikaraṇaṁ suvūpasantaṁ assāti.
pli-tv-kd14:14.25.17.eng: They thought, “If this senior monk resolves this legal issue—according to the Teaching, the Monastic Law, and the Teacher’s instruction—then it will be properly disposed of.”

pli-tv-kd14:14.25.18.pli: Atha kho te bhikkhū taṁ āvāsaṁ gantvā taṁ theraṁ etadavocuṁ—
pli-tv-kd14:14.25.18.eng: They then went to that monastery and said to that senior monk,

pli-tv-kd14:14.25.19.pli: “idaṁ, bhante, adhikaraṇaṁ evaṁ jātaṁ, evaṁ samuppannaṁ.
pli-tv-kd14:14.25.19.eng: “This legal issue has come about in this way.

pli-tv-kd14:14.25.20.pli: Sādhu, bhante, thero imaṁ adhikaraṇaṁ vūpasametu dhammena vinayena satthusāsanena, yathayidaṁ adhikaraṇaṁ suvūpasantaṁ assā”ti.
pli-tv-kd14:14.25.20.eng: Venerable, please resolve it—according to the Teaching, the Monastic Law, and the Teacher’s instruction—so that it will be properly disposed of.”

pli-tv-kd14:14.25.22.pli: yathā sāvatthiyā saṅghena adhikaraṇaṁ vūpasamitaṁ, yathā sambahulehi therehi adhikaraṇaṁ vūpasamitaṁ, yathā tīhi therehi adhikaraṇaṁ vūpasamitaṁ, yathā dvīhi therehi adhikaraṇaṁ vūpasamitaṁ, tathā suvūpasantanti—
pli-tv-kd14:14.25.22.eng: “This legal issue was properly disposed of by the Sangha at Sāvatthī, and likewise by that number of senior monks, by those three senior monks, and by those two senior monks,”

pli-tv-kd14:14.25.23.eng: and he disposed of it in the same way.

pli-tv-kd14:14.25.24.pli: Atha kho te bhikkhū asantuṭṭhā sāvatthiyā saṅghassa adhikaraṇavūpasamanena, asantuṭṭhā sambahulānaṁ therānaṁ adhikaraṇavūpasamanena, asantuṭṭhā tiṇṇaṁ therānaṁ adhikaraṇavūpasamanena, asantuṭṭhā dvinnaṁ therānaṁ adhikaraṇavūpasamanena, asantuṭṭhā ekassa therassa adhikaraṇavūpasamanena, yena bhagavā tenupasaṅkamiṁsu, upasaṅkamitvā bhagavato etamatthaṁ ārocesuṁ …pe…
pli-tv-kd14:14.25.24.eng: Since those monks were dissatisfied with how all of these had disposed of that legal issue, they went to the Buddha and told him what had happened. … The Buddha said:

pli-tv-kd14:14.25.25.eng: “Monks, this legal issue has been settled, laid to rest, and properly disposed of.

pli-tv-kd14:14.26.1.pli: Anujānāmi, bhikkhave, tesaṁ bhikkhūnaṁ saññattiyā tayo salākaggāhe—
pli-tv-kd14:14.26.1.eng: To persuade those monks, I allow three kinds of voting:

pli-tv-kd14:14.26.2.eng: a secret ballot, whispering in the ear, and an open vote.

pli-tv-kd14:14.26.4.pli: Tena salākaggāhāpakena bhikkhunā salākāyo vaṇṇāvaṇṇāyo katvā ekameko bhikkhu upasaṅkamitvā evamassa vacanīyo—
pli-tv-kd14:14.26.4.eng: The manager of the vote should make ballots of two different colors, go up to the monks one by one and say,

pli-tv-kd14:14.26.5.eng: ‘This is the ballot for those who hold this view, and this is the ballot for those who hold that view.

pli-tv-kd14:14.26.7.eng: When anyone has taken a ballot, they should be told,

pli-tv-kd14:14.26.10.eng: those who speak contrary to the Teaching are in the majority, then the vote is invalid and to be postponed.

pli-tv-kd14:14.26.12.eng: those who speak in accordance with the Teaching are in the majority, then the vote is valid and to be announced.

pli-tv-kd14:14.26.13.pli: Evaṁ kho, bhikkhave, gūḷhako salākaggāho hoti.

pli-tv-kd14:14.26.14.eng: What’s voting by whispering in the ear?

pli-tv-kd14:14.26.15.pli: Tena salākaggāhāpakena bhikkhunā ekamekassa bhikkhuno upakaṇṇake ārocetabbaṁ—
pli-tv-kd14:14.26.15.eng: The manager of the vote should inform the monks one by one by whispering in the ear,

pli-tv-kd14:14.26.16.eng: ‘This is the ballot for those who hold this view, and this is the ballot for those who hold that view.

pli-tv-kd14:14.26.18.eng: When someone has taken a ballot, they should be told,

pli-tv-kd14:14.26.21.eng: those who speak contrary to the Teaching are in the majority, then the vote is invalid and to be postponed.

pli-tv-kd14:14.26.23.eng: those who speak in accordance with the Teaching are in the majority, then the vote is valid and to be announced.

pli-tv-kd14:14.26.24.pli: Evaṁ kho, bhikkhave, sakaṇṇajappako salākaggāho hoti.

pli-tv-kd14:14.26.27.eng: those who speak in accordance with the Teaching are in the majority, the ballots should be distributed openly.”

pli-tv-kd14:14.26.28.pli: Evaṁ kho, bhikkhave, vivaṭako salākaggāho hoti.

pli-tv-kd14:14.27.2.eng: “There are four principles for settling a legal issue arising from an accusation:

pli-tv-kd14:14.27.3.pli: sammukhāvinayena ca, sativinayena ca, amūḷhavinayena ca, tassapāpiyasikāya ca.
pli-tv-kd14:14.27.3.eng: resolution face-to-face, resolution through recollection, resolution because of past insanity, and by further penalty.

pli-tv-kd14:14.27.4.eng: Is it possible that a legal issue arising from an accusation should be settled

pli-tv-kd14:14.27.5.eng: not by resolution because of past insanity or by further penalty,

pli-tv-kd14:14.27.7.eng: by resolution face-to-face and by resolution through recollection?

pli-tv-kd14:14.27.10.pli: Idha pana, bhikkhave, bhikkhū bhikkhuṁ amūlikāya sīlavipattiyā anuddhaṁsenti.
pli-tv-kd14:14.27.10.eng: It may be that monks are groundlessly charging a monk with failure in morality.

pli-tv-kd14:14.27.11.pli: Tassa kho, bhikkhave, bhikkhuno sativepullappattassa sativinayo dātabbo.
pli-tv-kd14:14.27.11.eng: If that monk has great clarity of memory, he’s to be granted resolution through recollection.

pli-tv-kd14:14.27.12.eng: And it should be granted like this.

pli-tv-kd14:14.27.13.pli: Tena, bhikkhave, bhikkhunā saṅghaṁ upasaṅkamitvā ekaṁsaṁ uttarāsaṅgaṁ karitvā …pe…
pli-tv-kd14:14.27.13.eng: That monk should approach the Sangha, arrange his upper robe over one shoulder, pay respect at the feet of the senior monks, squat on his heels, raise his joined palms,

pli-tv-kd14:14.27.15.eng: ‘Venerables, monks are groundlessly charging me with failure in morality.

pli-tv-kd14:14.27.16.pli: Sohaṁ, bhante, sativepullappatto saṅghaṁ sativinayaṁ yācāmī’ti.
pli-tv-kd14:14.27.16.eng: Because of my great clarity of memory, I ask the Sangha for resolution through recollection.’

pli-tv-kd14:14.27.17.eng: And he should ask a second

pli-tv-kd14:14.27.19.eng: A competent and capable monk should then inform the Sangha:

pli-tv-kd14:14.27.20.eng: ‘Please, venerables, I ask the Sangha to listen.
