pli-tv-kd14:14.27.21.pli: Bhikkhū itthannāmaṁ bhikkhuṁ amūlikāya sīlavipattiyā anuddhaṁsenti.
pli-tv-kd14:14.27.21.eng: Monks are groundlessly charging monk so-and-so with failure in morality.

pli-tv-kd14:14.27.22.eng: Because of his great clarity of memory, he’s asking the Sangha for resolution through recollection.

pli-tv-kd14:14.27.23.pli: Yadi saṅghassa pattakallaṁ, saṅgho itthannāmassa bhikkhuno sativepullappattassa sativinayaṁ dadeyya.
pli-tv-kd14:14.27.23.eng: If the Sangha is ready, it should grant him resolution through recollection.

pli-tv-kd14:14.27.25.eng: Please, venerables, I ask the Sangha to listen.

pli-tv-kd14:14.27.26.pli: Bhikkhū itthannāmaṁ bhikkhuṁ amūlikāya sīlavipattiyā anuddhaṁsenti.
pli-tv-kd14:14.27.26.eng: Monks are groundlessly charging monk so-and-so with failure in morality.

pli-tv-kd14:14.27.27.eng: Because of his great clarity of memory, he’s asking the Sangha for resolution through recollection.

pli-tv-kd14:14.27.28.pli: Saṅgho itthannāmassa bhikkhuno sativepullappattassa sativinayaṁ deti.
pli-tv-kd14:14.27.28.eng: The Sangha grants him resolution through recollection.

pli-tv-kd14:14.27.29.pli: Yassāyasmato khamati itthannāmassa bhikkhuno sativepullappattassa sativinayassa dānaṁ, so tuṇhassa;
pli-tv-kd14:14.27.29.eng: Any monk who approves of granting him resolution through recollection should remain silent.

pli-tv-kd14:14.27.30.eng: Any monk who doesn’t approve should speak up.

pli-tv-kd14:14.27.31.eng: For the second time, I speak on this matter. …

pli-tv-kd14:14.27.32.eng: For the third time, I speak on this matter. …

pli-tv-kd14:14.27.33.pli: Dinno saṅghena itthannāmassa bhikkhuno sativepullappattassa sativinayo.
pli-tv-kd14:14.27.33.eng: Because of his great clarity of memory, the Sangha has granted monk so-and-so resolution through recollection.

pli-tv-kd14:14.27.34.pli: Khamati saṅghassa, tasmā tuṇhī, evametaṁ dhārayāmī’ti.
pli-tv-kd14:14.27.34.eng: The Sangha approves and is therefore silent. I’ll remember it thus.’

pli-tv-kd14:14.27.35.eng: This is called the resolution of a legal issue.

pli-tv-kd14:14.27.37.eng: It’s been resolved by resolution face-to-face and by resolution through recollection.

pli-tv-kd14:14.27.39.eng: Face-to-face with the Sangha, the Teaching, the Monastic Law, and the persons concerned. This is the meaning of face-to-face with the Sangha: the monks who should be present have arrived, consent has been brought for those who are eligible to give their consent, and no-one present objects to the decision. This is the meaning of face-to-face with the Teaching and the Monastic Law: the Teaching, the Monastic Law, the Teacher’s instruction—that by which that legal issue is resolved.

pli-tv-kd14:14.27.40.eng: This is the meaning of face-to-face with the persons concerned:

pli-tv-kd14:14.27.41.pli: Yo ca anuvadati, yañca anuvadati, ubho sammukhībhūtā honti—
pli-tv-kd14:14.27.41.eng: both those who are accusing and those who have been accused are present.

pli-tv-kd14:14.27.43.eng: This is the meaning of resolution through recollection:

pli-tv-kd14:14.27.44.pli: Yā sativinayassa kammassa kiriyā karaṇaṁ upagamanaṁ ajjhupagamanaṁ adhivāsanā appaṭikkosanā—
pli-tv-kd14:14.27.44.eng: the doing of, the performing of, the participation in, the consent to, the agreement to, the non-objection to that legal procedure of resolution through recollection.

pli-tv-kd14:14.27.46.pli: Evaṁ vūpasantañce, bhikkhave, adhikaraṇaṁ kārako ukkoṭeti, ukkoṭanakaṁ pācittiyaṁ;
pli-tv-kd14:14.27.46.eng: When a legal issue has been resolved like this, if any of the participants reopen it, they incur an offense entailing confession for the reopening.

pli-tv-kd14:14.27.47.eng: If anyone who gave their consent criticizes the resolution, they incur an offense entailing confession.”

pli-tv-kd14:14.28.1.eng: “Is it possible that a legal issue arising from an accusation should be settled

pli-tv-kd14:14.28.2.eng: not by resolution through recollection or by further penalty,

pli-tv-kd14:14.28.4.eng: by resolution face-to-face and by resolution because of past insanity?

pli-tv-kd14:14.28.7.pli: Idha pana, bhikkhave, bhikkhu ummattako hoti cittavipariyāsakato.
pli-tv-kd14:14.28.7.eng: It may be that a monk is insane and suffering from psychosis.

pli-tv-kd14:14.28.8.pli: Tena ummattakena cittavipariyāsakatena bahuṁ assāmaṇakaṁ ajjhāciṇṇaṁ hoti bhāsitaparikkantaṁ.
pli-tv-kd14:14.28.8.eng: Because of that, he does and says many things unworthy of a monastic.

pli-tv-kd14:14.28.9.pli: Taṁ bhikkhū ummattakena cittavipariyāsakatena ajjhāciṇṇena āpattiyā codenti—
pli-tv-kd14:14.28.9.eng: Monks accuse him of an offense, saying,

pli-tv-kd14:14.28.10.eng: ‘Venerable, do you remember committing such-and-such an offense?’

pli-tv-kd14:14.28.12.eng: ‘I was insane and suffering from psychosis.

pli-tv-kd14:14.28.13.pli: Tena me ummattakena cittavipariyāsakatena bahuṁ assāmaṇakaṁ ajjhāciṇṇaṁ bhāsitaparikkantaṁ.
pli-tv-kd14:14.28.13.eng: Because of that, I did and said many things unworthy of a monastic.

pli-tv-kd14:14.28.15.eng: I did it because I was insane.’

pli-tv-kd14:14.28.18.pli: Tassa kho, bhikkhave, bhikkhuno amūḷhassa amūḷhavinayo dātabbo.
pli-tv-kd14:14.28.18.eng: If he’s no longer insane, that monk should be granted resolution because of past insanity.

pli-tv-kd14:14.28.19.eng: And it should be granted like this.

pli-tv-kd14:14.28.20.pli: Tena, bhikkhave, bhikkhunā saṅghaṁ upasaṅkamitvā ekaṁsaṁ uttarāsaṅgaṁ karitvā …pe…
pli-tv-kd14:14.28.20.eng: That monk should approach the Sangha, arrange his upper robe over one shoulder, pay respect at the feet of the senior monks, squat on his heels, raise his joined palms,

pli-tv-kd14:14.28.22.eng: ‘Venerables, I’ve been insane and suffering from psychosis.

pli-tv-kd14:14.28.23.pli: Tena me ummattakena cittavipariyāsakatena bahuṁ assāmaṇakaṁ ajjhāciṇṇaṁ bhāsitaparikkantaṁ.
pli-tv-kd14:14.28.23.eng: Because of that, I did and said many things unworthy of a monastic.

pli-tv-kd14:14.28.24.pli: Maṁ bhikkhū ummattakena cittavipariyāsakatena ajjhāciṇṇena āpattiyā codenti—
pli-tv-kd14:14.28.24.eng: Monks accused me of an offense, saying,

pli-tv-kd14:14.28.25.eng: “Venerable, do you remember committing such-and-such an offense?”

pli-tv-kd14:14.28.27.eng: “I was insane and suffering from psychosis.

pli-tv-kd14:14.28.28.pli: Tena me ummattakena cittavipariyāsakatena bahuṁ assāmaṇakaṁ ajjhāciṇṇaṁ bhāsitaparikkantaṁ.
pli-tv-kd14:14.28.28.eng: Because of that, I did and said many things unworthy of a monastic.

pli-tv-kd14:14.28.30.eng: I did it because I was insane.”

pli-tv-kd14:14.28.33.eng: Because I’m no longer insane, I ask the Sangha for resolution because of past insanity.’

pli-tv-kd14:14.28.34.eng: And he should ask a second

pli-tv-kd14:14.28.36.eng: A competent and capable monk should then inform the Sangha:

pli-tv-kd14:14.28.37.eng: ‘Please, venerables, I ask the Sangha to listen.

pli-tv-kd14:14.28.38.pli: Ayaṁ itthannāmo bhikkhu ummattako ahosi cittavipariyāsakato.
pli-tv-kd14:14.28.38.eng: The monk so-and-so has been insane and suffering from psychosis.

pli-tv-kd14:14.28.39.pli: Tena ummattakena cittavipariyāsakatena bahuṁ assāmaṇakaṁ ajjhāciṇṇaṁ bhāsitaparikkantaṁ.
pli-tv-kd14:14.28.39.eng: Because of that, he did and said many things unworthy of a monastic.

pli-tv-kd14:14.28.40.pli: Taṁ bhikkhū ummattakena cittavipariyāsakatena ajjhāciṇṇena āpattiyā codenti—
pli-tv-kd14:14.28.40.eng: Monks accused him of an offense, saying,

pli-tv-kd14:14.28.41.eng: “Venerable, do you remember committing such-and-such an offense?”

pli-tv-kd14:14.28.43.eng: “I was insane and suffering from psychosis.

pli-tv-kd14:14.28.44.pli: Tena me ummattakena cittavipariyāsakatena bahuṁ assāmaṇakaṁ ajjhāciṇṇaṁ bhāsitaparikkantaṁ.
pli-tv-kd14:14.28.44.eng: Because of that, I did and said many things unworthy of a monastic.

pli-tv-kd14:14.28.46.eng: I did it because I was insane.”

pli-tv-kd14:14.28.49.eng: Because he’s no longer insane, he’s asking the Sangha for resolution because of past insanity.

pli-tv-kd14:14.28.50.pli: Yadi saṅghassa pattakallaṁ, saṅgho itthannāmassa bhikkhuno amūḷhassa amūḷhavinayaṁ dadeyya.
pli-tv-kd14:14.28.50.eng: If the Sangha is ready, it should grant monk so-and-so resolution because of past insanity.

pli-tv-kd14:14.28.52.eng: Please, venerables, I ask the Sangha to listen.

pli-tv-kd14:14.28.53.pli: Ayaṁ itthannāmo bhikkhu ummattako ahosi cittavipariyāsakato.
pli-tv-kd14:14.28.53.eng: The monk so-and-so has been insane and suffering from psychosis.

pli-tv-kd14:14.28.54.pli: Tena ummattakena cittavipariyāsakatena bahuṁ assāmaṇakaṁ ajjhāciṇṇaṁ bhāsitaparikkantaṁ.
pli-tv-kd14:14.28.54.eng: Because of that, he did and said many things unworthy of a monastic.

pli-tv-kd14:14.28.55.pli: Taṁ bhikkhū ummattakena cittavipariyāsakatena ajjhāciṇṇena āpattiyā codenti—
pli-tv-kd14:14.28.55.eng: Monks accused him of an offense, saying,

pli-tv-kd14:14.28.56.eng: “Venerable, do you remember committing such-and-such an offense?”

pli-tv-kd14:14.28.58.eng: “I was insane and suffering from psychosis.

pli-tv-kd14:14.28.59.pli: Tena me ummattakena cittavipariyāsakatena bahuṁ assāmaṇakaṁ ajjhāciṇṇaṁ bhāsitaparikkantaṁ.
pli-tv-kd14:14.28.59.eng: Because of that, I did and said many things unworthy of a monastic.

pli-tv-kd14:14.28.61.eng: I did it because I was insane.”

pli-tv-kd14:14.28.64.eng: Because he’s no longer insane, he’s asking the Sangha for resolution because of past insanity.

pli-tv-kd14:14.28.65.pli: Saṅgho itthannāmassa bhikkhuno amūḷhassa amūḷhavinayaṁ deti.
pli-tv-kd14:14.28.65.eng: The Sangha grants monk so-and-so resolution because of past insanity.

pli-tv-kd14:14.28.66.pli: Yassāyasmato khamati itthannāmassa bhikkhuno amūḷhassa amūḷhavinayassa dānaṁ, so tuṇhassa;
pli-tv-kd14:14.28.66.eng: Any monk who approves of granting monk so-and-so resolution because of past insanity should remain silent.

pli-tv-kd14:14.28.67.eng: Any monk who doesn’t approve should speak up.

pli-tv-kd14:14.28.68.eng: For the second time, I speak on this matter. …

pli-tv-kd14:14.28.69.eng: For the third time, I speak on this matter. …

pli-tv-kd14:14.28.70.pli: Dinno saṅghena itthannāmassa bhikkhuno amūḷhassa amūḷhavinayo.
pli-tv-kd14:14.28.70.eng: Because he’s no longer insane, the Sangha has granted monk so-and-so resolution because of past insanity.

pli-tv-kd14:14.28.71.pli: Khamati saṅghassa, tasmā tuṇhī, evametaṁ dhārayāmī’ti.
pli-tv-kd14:14.28.71.eng: The Sangha approves and is therefore silent. I’ll remember it thus.’

pli-tv-kd14:14.28.72.eng: This is called the resolution of a legal issue.

pli-tv-kd14:14.28.74.eng: It’s been resolved by resolution face-to-face and by resolution because of past insanity.

pli-tv-kd14:14.28.76.eng: Face-to-face with the Sangha, the Teaching, the Monastic Law, and the persons concerned. This is the meaning of face-to-face with the Sangha: the monks who should be present have arrived, consent has been brought for those who are eligible to give their consent, and no-one present objects to the decision. This is the meaning of face-to-face with the Teaching and the Monastic Law: the Teaching, the Monastic Law, the Teacher’s instruction—that by which that legal issue is resolved. This is the meaning of face-to-face with the persons concerned: both those who are accusing and those who have been accused are present.

pli-tv-kd14:14.28.77.eng: This is the meaning of resolution because of past insanity:

pli-tv-kd14:14.28.78.pli: Yā amūḷhavinayassa kammassa kiriyā karaṇaṁ upagamanaṁ ajjhupagamanaṁ adhivāsanā appaṭikkosanā—
pli-tv-kd14:14.28.78.eng: the doing of, the performing of, the participation in, the consent to, the agreement to, the non-objection to that legal procedure of resolution because of past insanity.

pli-tv-kd14:14.28.80.pli: Evaṁ vūpasantañce, bhikkhave, adhikaraṇaṁ kārako ukkoṭeti, ukkoṭanakaṁ pācittiyaṁ;
pli-tv-kd14:14.28.80.eng: When a legal issue has been resolved like this, if any of the participants reopen it, they incur an offense entailing confession for the reopening.

pli-tv-kd14:14.28.81.eng: If anyone who gave their consent criticizes the resolution, they incur an offense entailing confession.”

pli-tv-kd14:14.29.1.eng: “Is it possible that a legal issue arising from an accusation should be settled

pli-tv-kd14:14.29.2.eng: not by resolution through recollection or by resolution because of past insanity,

pli-tv-kd14:14.29.4.eng: by resolution face-to-face and by further penalty?

pli-tv-kd14:14.29.7.pli: Idha pana, bhikkhave, bhikkhu bhikkhuṁ saṅghamajjhe garukāya āpattiyā codeti—
pli-tv-kd14:14.29.7.eng: It may be that a monk accuses a monk of an offense in the midst of the Sangha:

pli-tv-kd14:14.29.8.pli: ‘saratāyasmā evarūpiṁ garukaṁ āpattiṁ āpajjitā, pārājikaṁ vā pārājikasāmantaṁ vā’ti.
pli-tv-kd14:14.29.8.eng: ‘Venerable, do you remember committing such-and-such a heavy offense entailing expulsion or bordering on expulsion?’

pli-tv-kd14:14.29.10.pli: ‘na kho ahaṁ, āvuso, sarāmi evarūpiṁ garukaṁ āpattiṁ āpajjitā, pārājikaṁ vā pārājikasāmantaṁ vā’ti.

pli-tv-kd14:14.29.11.eng: As he tries to free himself, the accusing monk presses him further:

pli-tv-kd14:14.29.12.pli: ‘iṅghāyasmā sādhukameva jānāhi, yadi sarasi evarūpiṁ garukaṁ āpattiṁ āpajjitā, pārājikaṁ vā pārājikasāmantaṁ vā’ti.
pli-tv-kd14:14.29.12.eng: ‘Come on, venerable, try again to  remember whether you’ve committed such-and-such a heavy offense.’

pli-tv-kd14:14.29.14.pli: ‘na kho ahaṁ, āvuso, sarāmi evarūpiṁ garukaṁ āpattiṁ āpajjitā, pārājikaṁ vā pārājikasāmantaṁ vā.
pli-tv-kd14:14.29.14.eng: ‘I don’t remember committing such an offense,

pli-tv-kd14:14.29.15.pli: Sarāmi ca kho ahaṁ, āvuso, evarūpiṁ appamattikaṁ āpattiṁ āpajjitā’ti.
pli-tv-kd14:14.29.15.eng: but I do remember committing such-and-such a minor offense.’

pli-tv-kd14:14.29.16.eng: As he tries to free himself, the accusing monk presses him further:

pli-tv-kd14:14.29.17.pli: ‘iṅghāyasmā sādhukameva jānāhi, yadi sarasi evarūpiṁ garukaṁ āpattiṁ āpajjitā, pārājikaṁ vā pārājikasāmantaṁ vā’ti.
pli-tv-kd14:14.29.17.eng: ‘Come on, venerable, try harder to  remember whether you’ve committed such-and-such a heavy offense.’

pli-tv-kd14:14.29.19.pli: ‘imañhi nāmāhaṁ, āvuso, appamattikaṁ āpattiṁ āpajjitvā apuṭṭho paṭijānissāmi.
pli-tv-kd14:14.29.19.eng: ‘I’ve admitted that I’ve committed this minor offense without being asked.

pli-tv-kd14:14.29.20.pli: Kiṁ panāhaṁ evarūpiṁ garukaṁ āpattiṁ āpajjitvā, pārājikaṁ vā pārājikasāmantaṁ vā, puṭṭho na paṭijānissāmī’ti?
pli-tv-kd14:14.29.20.eng: So when asked about a heavy offense, why wouldn’t I admit it?’

pli-tv-kd14:14.29.22.pli: ‘imañhi nāma tvaṁ, āvuso, appamattikaṁ āpattiṁ āpajjitvā apuṭṭho na paṭijānissasi.
pli-tv-kd14:14.29.22.eng: ‘But you didn’t admit that you had committed this minor offense without being asked.

pli-tv-kd14:14.29.23.pli: Kiṁ pana tvaṁ evarūpiṁ garukaṁ āpattiṁ āpajjitvā, pārājikaṁ vā pārājikasāmantaṁ vā, apuṭṭho paṭijānissasi?
pli-tv-kd14:14.29.23.eng: So when asked about a heavy offense, why would you admit it?

pli-tv-kd14:14.29.24.pli: Iṅghāyasmā sādhukameva jānāhi, yadi sarasi evarūpiṁ garukaṁ āpattiṁ āpajjitā, pārājikaṁ vā pārājikasāmantaṁ vā’ti.
pli-tv-kd14:14.29.24.eng: Come on, venerable, try harder to  remember whether you’ve committed such-and-such a heavy offense.’

pli-tv-kd14:14.29.26.pli: ‘sarāmi kho ahaṁ, āvuso, evarūpiṁ garukaṁ āpattiṁ āpajjitā, pārājikaṁ vā pārājikasāmantaṁ vā.
pli-tv-kd14:14.29.26.eng: ‘I remember committing such-and-such a heavy offense entailing expulsion or bordering on expulsion.

pli-tv-kd14:14.29.27.pli: Davā me etaṁ vuttaṁ, ravā me etaṁ vuttaṁ—
pli-tv-kd14:14.29.27.eng: When I said that I didn’t remember, I spoke playfully, I spoke too fast.’

pli-tv-kd14:14.29.28.pli: nāhaṁ taṁ sarāmi evarūpiṁ garukaṁ āpattiṁ āpajjitā, pārājikaṁ vā pārājikasāmantaṁ vā’ti.

pli-tv-kd14:14.29.29.pli: Tassa kho, bhikkhave, bhikkhuno tassapāpiyasikākammaṁ kātabbaṁ.
pli-tv-kd14:14.29.29.eng: They should do a legal procedure of further penalty against that monk.

pli-tv-kd14:14.29.30.eng: And it should be done like this.

pli-tv-kd14:14.29.31.eng: A competent and capable monk should inform the Sangha:

pli-tv-kd14:14.29.32.eng: ‘Please, venerables, I ask the Sangha to listen.

pli-tv-kd14:14.29.33.pli: Ayaṁ itthannāmo bhikkhu saṅghamajjhe garukāya āpattiyā anuyuñjiyamāno avajānitvā paṭijānāti, paṭijānitvā avajānāti, aññenāññaṁ paṭicarati, sampajānamusā bhāsati.
pli-tv-kd14:14.29.33.eng: The monk so-and-so, while being examined in the midst of the Sangha about a heavy offense, asserts things after denying them, denies things after asserting them, evades the issue, and lies.

pli-tv-kd14:14.29.34.pli: Yadi saṅghassa pattakallaṁ, saṅgho itthannāmassa bhikkhuno tassapāpiyasikākammaṁ kareyya.
pli-tv-kd14:14.29.34.eng: If the Sangha is ready, it should do a legal procedure of further penalty against monk so-and-so.

pli-tv-kd14:14.29.36.eng: Please, venerables, I ask the Sangha to listen.

pli-tv-kd14:14.29.37.pli: Ayaṁ itthannāmo bhikkhu saṅghamajjhe garukāya āpattiyā anuyuñjiyamāno avajānitvā paṭijānāti, paṭijānitvā avajānāti, aññenāññaṁ paṭicarati, sampajānamusā bhāsati.
pli-tv-kd14:14.29.37.eng: The monk so-and-so, while being examined in the midst of the Sangha about a heavy offense, asserts things after denying them, denies things after asserting them, evades the issue, and lies.

pli-tv-kd14:14.29.38.eng: The Sangha does a legal procedure of further penalty against monk so-and-so.

pli-tv-kd14:14.29.39.pli: Yassāyasmato khamati itthannāmassa bhikkhuno tassapāpiyasikākammassa karaṇaṁ, so tuṇhassa;
pli-tv-kd14:14.29.39.eng: Any monk who approves of doing a legal procedure of further penalty against monk so-and-so should remain silent.

pli-tv-kd14:14.29.40.eng: Any monk who doesn’t approve should speak up.

pli-tv-kd14:14.29.41.eng: For the second time, I speak on this matter. …

pli-tv-kd14:14.29.42.eng: For the third time, I speak on this matter. …

pli-tv-kd14:14.29.43.eng: The Sangha has done a legal procedure of further penalty against monk so-and-so.

pli-tv-kd14:14.29.44.pli: Khamati saṅghassa, tasmā tuṇhī, evametaṁ dhārayāmī’ti.
pli-tv-kd14:14.29.44.eng: The Sangha approves and is therefore silent. I’ll remember it thus.’

pli-tv-kd14:14.29.45.eng: This is called the resolution of a legal issue.

pli-tv-kd14:14.29.47.eng: It’s been resolved face-to-face and by further penalty.

pli-tv-kd14:14.29.49.eng: Face-to-face with the Sangha, the Teaching, the Monastic Law, and the persons concerned. This is the meaning of face-to-face with the Sangha: the monks who should be present have arrived, consent has been brought for those who are eligible to give their consent, and no-one present objects to the decision. This is the meaning of face-to-face with the Teaching and the Monastic Law: the Teaching, the Monastic Law, the Teacher’s instruction—that by which that legal issue is resolved. This is the meaning of face-to-face with the persons concerned: both those who are accusing and those who have been accused are present.

pli-tv-kd14:14.29.50.eng: This is the meaning of ‘by further penalty’:

pli-tv-kd14:14.29.51.pli: Yā tassapāpiyasikākammassa kiriyā karaṇaṁ upagamanaṁ ajjhupagamanaṁ adhivāsanā appaṭikkosanā—
pli-tv-kd14:14.29.51.eng: the doing of, the performing of, the participation in, the consent to, the agreement to, the non-objection to that legal procedure of further penalty.

pli-tv-kd14:14.29.53.pli: Evaṁ vūpasantañce, bhikkhave, adhikaraṇaṁ kārako ukkoṭeti, ukkoṭanakaṁ pācittiyaṁ;
pli-tv-kd14:14.29.53.eng: When a legal issue has been resolved like this, if any of the participants reopen it, they incur an offense entailing confession for the reopening.

pli-tv-kd14:14.29.54.eng: If anyone who gave their consent criticizes the resolution, they incur an offense entailing confession.”

pli-tv-kd14:14.30.0.eng: Acting according to what has been admitted

pli-tv-kd14:14.30.2.eng: “There are three principles for settling a legal issue arising from an offense:

pli-tv-kd14:14.30.3.pli: sammukhāvinayena ca, paṭiññātakaraṇena ca, tiṇavatthārakena ca.
pli-tv-kd14:14.30.3.eng: resolution face-to-face, acting according to what’s been admitted, and covering over as if with grass.

pli-tv-kd14:14.30.4.eng: Is it possible that a legal issue arising from an accusation should be settled

pli-tv-kd14:14.30.5.eng: not by covering over as if with grass,

pli-tv-kd14:14.30.6.eng: but by resolution face-to-face and by acting according to what’s been admitted?

pli-tv-kd14:14.30.9.pli: Idha pana, bhikkhave, bhikkhu lahukaṁ āpattiṁ āpanno hoti.
pli-tv-kd14:14.30.9.eng: It may be that a monk has committed a light offense.

pli-tv-kd14:14.30.10.pli: Tena, bhikkhave, bhikkhunā ekaṁ bhikkhuṁ upasaṅkamitvā ekaṁsaṁ uttarāsaṅgaṁ karitvā ukkuṭikaṁ nisīditvā añjaliṁ paggahetvā evamassa vacanīyo—
pli-tv-kd14:14.30.10.eng: That monk should approach a single monk, arrange his upper robe over one shoulder, squat on his heels, raise his joined palms, and say:

pli-tv-kd14:14.30.16.eng: ‘You should restrain yourself in the future.’

pli-tv-kd14:14.30.17.eng: This is called the resolution of a legal issue.

pli-tv-kd14:14.30.19.eng: It’s been resolved face-to-face and by acting according to what’s been admitted.

pli-tv-kd14:14.30.21.eng: Face-to-face with the Teaching, the Monastic Law, and the persons concerned. This is the meaning of face-to-face with the Teaching and the Monastic Law: the Teaching, the Monastic Law, the Teacher’s instruction—that by which that legal issue is resolved.

pli-tv-kd14:14.30.22.eng: This is the meaning of face-to-face with the persons concerned:

pli-tv-kd14:14.30.23.pli: Yo ca deseti, yassa ca deseti, ubho sammukhībhūtā honti—
pli-tv-kd14:14.30.23.eng: both the one who confesses and the one he confesses to are present.

pli-tv-kd14:14.30.25.eng: This is the meaning of acting according to what’s been admitted:

pli-tv-kd14:14.30.26.pli: Yā paṭiññātakaraṇassa kammassa kiriyā karaṇaṁ upagamanaṁ ajjhupagamanaṁ adhivāsanā appaṭikkosanā—
pli-tv-kd14:14.30.26.eng: the doing of, the performing of, the participation in, the consent to, the agreement to, the non-objection to that legal procedure of acting according to what’s been admitted.

pli-tv-kd14:14.30.28.pli: Evaṁ vūpasantañce, bhikkhave, adhikaraṇaṁ paṭiggāhako ukkoṭeti, ukkoṭanakaṁ pācittiyaṁ.
pli-tv-kd14:14.30.28.eng: When a legal issue has been resolved like this, if the receiver of the confession reopens it, he incurs an offense entailing confession for the reopening.

pli-tv-kd14:14.31.1.eng: If this is what happens, all’s well.

pli-tv-kd14:14.31.2.pli: No ce labhetha, tena, bhikkhave, bhikkhunā sambahule bhikkhū upasaṅkamitvā ekaṁsaṁ uttarāsaṅgaṁ karitvā vuḍḍhānaṁ bhikkhūnaṁ pāde vanditvā ukkuṭikaṁ nisīditvā añjaliṁ paggahetvā evamassu vacanīyā—
pli-tv-kd14:14.31.2.eng: If not, that monk should approach several monks, arrange his upper robe over one shoulder, pay respect at the feet of the senior monks, squat on his heels, raise his joined palms, and say,

pli-tv-kd14:14.31.5.pli: Byattena bhikkhunā paṭibalena te bhikkhū ñāpetabbā—
pli-tv-kd14:14.31.5.eng: A competent and capable monk should then inform those monks:

pli-tv-kd14:14.31.6.eng: ‘Please, venerables, I ask you to listen.

pli-tv-kd14:14.31.7.pli: Ayaṁ itthannāmo bhikkhu āpattiṁ sarati, vivarati, uttāniṁ karoti deseti.
pli-tv-kd14:14.31.7.eng: The monk so-and-so remembers an offense—he reveals it, makes it plain, and confesses it.

pli-tv-kd14:14.31.8.pli: Yadāyasmantānaṁ pattakallaṁ, ahaṁ itthannāmassa bhikkhuno āpattiṁ paṭiggaṇheyyan’ti.
pli-tv-kd14:14.31.8.eng: If the venerables are ready, I’ll receive his confession.’

pli-tv-kd14:14.31.12.eng: ‘You should restrain yourself in the future.’

pli-tv-kd14:14.31.13.eng: This is called the resolution of a legal issue.

pli-tv-kd14:14.31.15.eng: It’s been resolved face-to-face and by acting according to what’s been admitted.

pli-tv-kd14:14.31.17.eng: Face-to-face with the Teaching, the Monastic Law, and the persons concerned. This is the meaning of face-to-face with the Teaching and the Monastic Law: the Teaching, the Monastic Law, the Teacher’s instruction—that by which that legal issue is resolved.

pli-tv-kd14:14.31.18.eng: This is the meaning of face-to-face with the persons concerned:

pli-tv-kd14:14.31.19.pli: Yo ca deseti, yassa ca deseti, ubho sammukhībhūtā honti—
pli-tv-kd14:14.31.19.eng: both the one who confesses and the one he confesses to are present.

pli-tv-kd14:14.31.21.eng: This is the meaning of acting according to what’s been admitted:

pli-tv-kd14:14.31.22.pli: Yā paṭiññātakaraṇassa kammassa kiriyā karaṇaṁ upagamanaṁ ajjhupagamanaṁ adhivāsanā appaṭikkosanā—
pli-tv-kd14:14.31.22.eng: the doing of, the performing of, the participation in, the consent to, the agreement to, the non-objection to that legal procedure of acting according to what’s been admitted.

pli-tv-kd14:14.31.24.pli: Evaṁ vūpasantañce, bhikkhave, adhikaraṇaṁ paṭiggāhako ukkoṭeti, ukkoṭanakaṁ pācittiyaṁ.
pli-tv-kd14:14.31.24.eng: When a legal issue has been resolved like this, if the receiver of the confession reopens it, he incurs an offense entailing confession for the reopening.

pli-tv-kd14:14.32.1.eng: If this is what happens, all’s well.

pli-tv-kd14:14.32.2.pli: No ce labhetha, tena, bhikkhave, bhikkhunā saṅghaṁ upasaṅkamitvā ekaṁsaṁ uttarāsaṅgaṁ karitvā vuḍḍhānaṁ bhikkhūnaṁ pāde vanditvā ukkuṭikaṁ nisīditvā añjaliṁ paggahetvā evamassa vacanīyo—
pli-tv-kd14:14.32.2.eng: If not, that monk should approach the Sangha, arrange his upper robe over one shoulder, pay respect at the feet of the senior monks, squat on his heels, raise his joined palms, and say,

pli-tv-kd14:14.32.5.eng: A competent and capable monk should then inform the Sangha:

pli-tv-kd14:14.32.6.eng: ‘Please, venerables, I ask the Sangha to listen.

pli-tv-kd14:14.32.7.pli: Ayaṁ itthannāmo bhikkhu āpattiṁ sarati, vivarati, uttāniṁ karoti, deseti.
pli-tv-kd14:14.32.7.eng: The monk so-and-so remembers an offense—he reveals it, makes it plain, and confesses it.

pli-tv-kd14:14.32.8.pli: Yadi saṅghassa pattakallaṁ, ahaṁ itthannāmassa bhikkhuno āpattiṁ paṭiggaṇheyyan’ti.
pli-tv-kd14:14.32.8.eng: If the Sangha is ready, I’ll receive his confession.’

pli-tv-kd14:14.32.12.eng: ‘You should restrain yourself in the future.’

pli-tv-kd14:14.32.13.eng: This is called the resolution of a legal issue.

pli-tv-kd14:14.32.15.eng: It’s been resolved face-to-face and by acting according to what’s been admitted.

pli-tv-kd14:14.32.17.eng: Face-to-face with the Sangha, the Teaching, the Monastic Law, and the persons concerned. This is the meaning of face-to-face with the Sangha: the monks who should be present have arrived, consent has been brought for those who are eligible to give their consent, and no-one present objects to the decision. This is the meaning of face-to-face with the Teaching and the Monastic Law: the Teaching, the Monastic Law, the Teacher’s instruction—that by which that legal issue is resolved. This is the meaning of face-to-face with the persons concerned: both the one who confesses and the one he confesses to are present. This is the meaning of acting according to what’s been admitted: the doing of, the performing of, the participation in, the consent to, the agreement to, the non-objection to that legal procedure of acting according to what’s been admitted.

pli-tv-kd14:14.32.18.pli: evaṁ vūpasantañce, bhikkhave, adhikaraṇaṁ paṭiggāhako ukkoṭeti, ukkoṭanakaṁ pācittiyaṁ;
pli-tv-kd14:14.32.18.eng: When a legal issue has been resolved like this, if the receiver of the confession reopens it, he incurs an offense entailing confession for the reopening.

pli-tv-kd14:14.32.19.eng: If anyone who gave their consent criticizes the resolution, they incur an offense entailing confession.”

pli-tv-kd14:14.33.0.eng: Covering over as if with grass

pli-tv-kd14:14.33.1.eng: “Is it possible that a legal issue arising from an offense should be settled

pli-tv-kd14:14.33.2.eng: not by acting according to what’s been admitted,

pli-tv-kd14:14.33.3.eng: but by resolution face-to-face and by covering over as if with grass?

pli-tv-kd14:14.33.6.pli: Idha pana, bhikkhave, bhikkhūnaṁ bhaṇḍanajātānaṁ kalahajātānaṁ vivādāpannānaṁ viharataṁ bahuṁ assāmaṇakaṁ ajjhāciṇṇaṁ hoti bhāsitaparikkantaṁ.
pli-tv-kd14:14.33.6.eng: It may be that monks who are arguing and disputing do and say many things unworthy of monastics.

pli-tv-kd14:14.33.7.eng: If they consider this and think,

pli-tv-kd14:14.33.8.pli: ‘amhākaṁ kho bhaṇḍanajātānaṁ kalahajātānaṁ vivādāpannānaṁ viharataṁ bahuṁ assāmaṇakaṁ ajjhāciṇṇaṁ bhāsitaparikkantaṁ.

pli-tv-kd14:14.33.9.pli: Sace mayaṁ imāhi āpattīhi aññamaññaṁ kāressāma, siyāpi taṁ adhikaraṇaṁ kakkhaḷattāya vāḷattāya bhedāya saṁvatteyyā’ti.
pli-tv-kd14:14.33.9.eng: ‘If we deal with one another for these offenses, this legal issue might lead to harshness, nastiness, and schism,’

pli-tv-kd14:14.33.10.pli: Anujānāmi, bhikkhave, evarūpaṁ adhikaraṇaṁ tiṇavatthārakena vūpasametuṁ.
pli-tv-kd14:14.33.10.eng: then I allow you to resolve this legal issue by covering over as if with grass.

pli-tv-kd14:14.33.11.eng: And it should be resolved like this.

pli-tv-kd14:14.33.12.pli: Sabbeheva ekajjhaṁ sannipatitabbaṁ, sannipatitvā byattena bhikkhunā paṭibalena saṅgho ñāpetabbo—
pli-tv-kd14:14.33.12.eng: Everyone should gather in one place. A competent and capable monk should then inform the Sangha:

pli-tv-kd14:14.33.13.eng: ‘Please, venerables, I ask the Sangha to listen.

pli-tv-kd14:14.33.14.pli: Amhākaṁ bhaṇḍanajātānaṁ kalahajātānaṁ vivādāpannānaṁ viharataṁ bahuṁ assāmaṇakaṁ ajjhāciṇṇaṁ bhāsitaparikkantaṁ.
pli-tv-kd14:14.33.14.eng: While we were arguing and disputing, we did and said many things unworthy of monastics.

pli-tv-kd14:14.33.15.pli: Sace mayaṁ imāhi āpattīhi aññamaññaṁ kāressāma, siyāpi taṁ adhikaraṇaṁ kakkhaḷattāya vāḷattāya bhedāya saṁvatteyya.
pli-tv-kd14:14.33.15.eng: If we deal with one another for these offenses, this legal issue might lead to harshness, nastiness, and schism.

pli-tv-kd14:14.33.16.pli: Yadi saṅghassa pattakallaṁ, saṅgho imaṁ adhikaraṇaṁ tiṇavatthārakena vūpasameyya, ṭhapetvā thullavajjaṁ, ṭhapetvā gihippaṭisaṁyuttanti.
pli-tv-kd14:14.33.16.eng: If the Sangha is ready, it should resolve this legal issue by covering over as if with grass, except for heavy offenses and offenses connected with householders.’

pli-tv-kd14:14.33.17.pli: Ekatopakkhikānaṁ bhikkhūnaṁ byattena bhikkhunā paṭibalena sako pakkho ñāpetabbo—
pli-tv-kd14:14.33.17.eng: The monks belonging to one side should be informed by a competent and capable monk belonging to their own side:

pli-tv-kd14:14.33.18.eng: ‘Please, venerables, I ask you to listen.

pli-tv-kd14:14.33.19.pli: Amhākaṁ bhaṇḍanajātānaṁ kalahajātānaṁ vivādāpannānaṁ viharataṁ bahuṁ assāmaṇakaṁ ajjhāciṇṇaṁ bhāsitaparikkantaṁ.
pli-tv-kd14:14.33.19.eng: While we were arguing and disputing, we did and said many things unworthy of monastics.

pli-tv-kd14:14.33.20.pli: Sace mayaṁ imāhi āpattīhi aññamaññaṁ kāressāma, siyāpi taṁ adhikaraṇaṁ kakkhaḷattāya vāḷattāya bhedāya saṁvatteyya.
pli-tv-kd14:14.33.20.eng: If we deal with one another for these offenses, this legal issue might lead to harshness, nastiness, and schism.

pli-tv-kd14:14.33.21.pli: Yadāyasmantānaṁ pattakallaṁ, ahaṁ yā ceva āyasmantānaṁ āpatti, yā ca attano āpatti, āyasmantānañceva atthāya, attano ca atthāya, saṅghamajjhe tiṇavatthārakena deseyyaṁ, ṭhapetvā thullavajjaṁ, ṭhapetvā gihippaṭisaṁyuttan’ti.
pli-tv-kd14:14.33.21.eng: If the venerables are ready, then for your benefit and for my own, I’ll confess in the midst of the Sangha both your and my own offenses by covering over as if with grass, except for heavy offenses and offenses connected with householders.’

pli-tv-kd14:14.33.22.pli: Athāparesaṁ ekatopakkhikānaṁ bhikkhūnaṁ byattena bhikkhunā paṭibalena sako pakkho ñāpetabbo—
pli-tv-kd14:14.33.22.eng: The monks belonging to the other side should be informed by a competent and capable monk belonging to their own side:

pli-tv-kd14:14.33.23.eng: ‘Please, venerables, I ask you to listen.

pli-tv-kd14:14.33.24.pli: Amhākaṁ bhaṇḍanajātānaṁ kalahajātānaṁ vivādāpannānaṁ viharataṁ bahuṁ assāmaṇakaṁ ajjhāciṇṇaṁ bhāsitaparikkantaṁ.
pli-tv-kd14:14.33.24.eng: While we were arguing and disputing, we did and said many things unworthy of monastics.

pli-tv-kd14:14.33.25.pli: Sace mayaṁ imāhi āpattīhi aññamaññaṁ kāressāma, siyāpi taṁ adhikaraṇaṁ kakkhaḷattāya vāḷattāya bhedāya saṁvatteyya.
pli-tv-kd14:14.33.25.eng: If we deal with one another for these offenses, this legal issue might lead to harshness, nastiness, and schism.

pli-tv-kd14:14.33.26.pli: Yadāyasmantānaṁ pattakallaṁ, ahaṁ yā ceva āyasmantānaṁ āpatti, yā ca attano āpatti, āyasmantānañceva atthāya, attano ca atthāya, saṅghamajjhe tiṇavatthārakena deseyyaṁ, ṭhapetvā thullavajjaṁ, ṭhapetvā gihippaṭisaṁyuttan’ti.
pli-tv-kd14:14.33.26.eng: If the venerables are ready, then for your benefit and for my own, I’ll confess in the midst of the Sangha both your and my own offenses by covering over as if with grass, except for heavy offenses and offenses connected with householders.’

pli-tv-kd14:14.33.27.pli: Athāparesaṁ ekatopakkhikānaṁ bhikkhūnaṁ byattena bhikkhunā paṭibalena saṅgho ñāpetabbo—
pli-tv-kd14:14.33.27.eng: A competent and capable monk belonging to one side should inform the Sangha:
pli-tv-kd14:14.33.27.comm: I here follow the PTS reading, ekatopakkhikānaṁ bhikkhūnaṁ, as opposed to the MS reading, athāparesaṁ ekatopakkhikānaṁ bhikkhūnaṁ. With the MS reading there is no distinction between the two groups of monks. The PTS reading is supported by the reading in OPM.

pli-tv-kd14:14.33.28.eng: ‘Please, venerables, I ask the Sangha to listen.

pli-tv-kd14:14.33.29.pli: Amhākaṁ bhaṇḍanajātānaṁ kalahajātānaṁ vivādāpannānaṁ viharataṁ bahuṁ assāmaṇakaṁ ajjhāciṇṇaṁ bhāsitaparikkantaṁ.
pli-tv-kd14:14.33.29.eng: While we were arguing and disputing, we did and said many things unworthy of monastics.

pli-tv-kd14:14.33.30.pli: Sace mayaṁ imāhi āpattīhi aññamaññaṁ kāressāma, siyāpi taṁ adhikaraṇaṁ kakkhaḷattāya vāḷattāya bhedāya saṁvatteyya.
pli-tv-kd14:14.33.30.eng: If we deal with one another for these offenses, this legal issue might lead to harshness, nastiness, and schism.

pli-tv-kd14:14.33.31.pli: Yadi saṅghassa pattakallaṁ, ahaṁ yā ceva imesaṁ āyasmantānaṁ āpatti, yā ca attano āpatti, imesañceva āyasmantānaṁ atthāya, attano ca atthāya, saṅghamajjhe tiṇavatthārakena deseyyaṁ, ṭhapetvā thullavajjaṁ, ṭhapetvā gihippaṭisaṁyuttaṁ.
pli-tv-kd14:14.33.31.eng: If the Sangha is ready, then for the benefit of these venerables and myself, I’ll confess in the midst of the Sangha both their and my own offenses by covering over as if with grass, except for heavy offenses and offenses connected with householders.

pli-tv-kd14:14.33.33.eng: ‘Please, venerables, I ask the Sangha to listen.

pli-tv-kd14:14.33.34.pli: Amhākaṁ bhaṇḍanajātānaṁ kalahajātānaṁ vivādāpannānaṁ viharataṁ bahuṁ assāmaṇakaṁ ajjhāciṇṇaṁ bhāsitaparikkantaṁ.
pli-tv-kd14:14.33.34.eng: While we were arguing and disputing, we did and said many things unworthy of monastics.

pli-tv-kd14:14.33.35.pli: Sace mayaṁ imāhi āpattīhi aññamaññaṁ kāressāma, siyāpi taṁ adhikaraṇaṁ kakkhaḷattāya vāḷattāya bhedāya saṁvatteyya.
pli-tv-kd14:14.33.35.eng: If we deal with one another for these offenses, this legal issue might lead to harshness, nastiness, and schism.

pli-tv-kd14:14.33.36.pli: Ahaṁ yā ceva imesaṁ āyasmantānaṁ āpatti, yā ca attano āpatti, imesañceva āyasmantānaṁ atthāya, attano ca atthāya, saṅghamajjhe tiṇavatthārakena desemi, ṭhapetvā thullavajjaṁ, ṭhapetvā gihippaṭisaṁyuttaṁ.
pli-tv-kd14:14.33.36.eng: For the benefit of these venerables and myself, I confess both their and my own offenses in the midst of the Sangha by covering over as if with grass, except for heavy offenses and offenses connected with householders.

pli-tv-kd14:14.33.37.pli: Yassāyasmato khamati amhākaṁ imāsaṁ āpattīnaṁ saṅghamajjhe tiṇavatthārakena desanā, ṭhapetvā thullavajjaṁ, ṭhapetvā gihippaṭisaṁyuttaṁ, so tuṇhassa;
pli-tv-kd14:14.33.37.eng: Any monk who approves of confessing our offenses in the midst of the Sangha by covering over as if with grass should remain silent.

pli-tv-kd14:14.33.38.eng: Any monk who doesn’t approve should speak up.

pli-tv-kd14:14.33.39.pli: Desitā amhākaṁ imā āpattiyo saṅghamajjhe tiṇavatthārakena, ṭhapetvā thullavajjaṁ, ṭhapetvā gihippaṭisaṁyuttaṁ.
pli-tv-kd14:14.33.39.eng: We have confessed our offenses in the midst of the Sangha by covering over as if with grass, except for heavy offenses and offenses connected with householders.

pli-tv-kd14:14.33.40.pli: Khamati saṅghassa, tasmā tuṇhī, evametaṁ dhārayāmī’ti.
pli-tv-kd14:14.33.40.eng: The Sangha approves and is therefore silent. I’ll remember it thus.’

pli-tv-kd14:14.33.41.1.eng: A competent and capable monk belonging to the other side should inform the Sangha:

pli-tv-kd14:14.33.41.2.eng: ‘Please, Venerables, I ask the Sangha to listen. While we were arguing and disputing, we did and said many things unworthy of monastics. If we deal with one another for these offenses, this legal issue might lead to harshness, nastiness, and schism. If it seems appropriate to the Sangha, then for the benefit of these venerables and myself, I’ll confess in the midst of the Sangha both their and my own offenses by covering over as if with grass, except for heavy offenses and offenses connected with householders. This is the motion.

pli-tv-kd14:14.33.41.3.eng: ‘Please, Venerables, I ask the Sangha to listen. While we were arguing and disputing, we did and said many things unworthy of monastics. If we deal with one another for these offenses, this legal issue might lead to harshness, nastiness, and schism. For the benefit of these venerables and myself, I confess in the midst of the Sangha both their and my own offenses by covering over, as if with grass, except for heavy offenses and offenses connected with householders. Any monk who approves of confessing our offenses in the midst of the Sangha by covering over as if with grass should remain silent. Any monk who doesn’t approve should speak up.

pli-tv-kd14:14.33.41.4.eng: We have confessed our offenses in the midst of the Sangha by covering over as if with grass, except for heavy offenses and offenses connected with householders. The Sangha approves and is therefore silent.

pli-tv-kd14:14.33.43.eng: This is called the resolution of a legal issue.

pli-tv-kd14:14.33.45.eng: It’s been resolved face-to-face and by covering over as if with grass.

pli-tv-kd14:14.33.47.eng: Face-to-face with the Sangha, the Teaching, the Monastic Law, and the persons concerned.

pli-tv-kd14:14.33.48.eng: This is the meaning of face-to-face with the Sangha:

pli-tv-kd14:14.33.49.pli: Yāvatikā bhikkhū kammappattā, te āgatā honti, chandārahānaṁ chando āhaṭo hoti, sammukhībhūtā na paṭikkosanti—
pli-tv-kd14:14.33.49.eng: the monks who should be present have arrived, consent has been brought for those who are eligible to give their consent, and no-one present objects to the decision.

pli-tv-kd14:14.33.51.eng: This is the meaning of face-to-face with the Teaching and the Monastic Law:

pli-tv-kd14:14.33.52.pli: Yena dhammena yena vinayena yena satthusāsanena taṁ adhikaraṇaṁ vūpasammati—
pli-tv-kd14:14.33.52.eng: the Teaching, the Monastic Law, the Teacher’s instruction—that by which that legal issue is resolved.

pli-tv-kd14:14.33.54.eng: This is the meaning of face-to-face with the persons concerned:

pli-tv-kd14:14.33.55.pli: Yo ca deseti, yassa ca deseti, ubho sammukhībhūtā honti—
pli-tv-kd14:14.33.55.eng: both the one who confesses and the one he confesses to are present.
pli-tv-kd14:14.33.55.comm: Here “the one he confesses to” should presumably be seen as the Sangha.

pli-tv-kd14:14.33.57.eng: This is the meaning of covering over as if with grass:

pli-tv-kd14:14.33.58.pli: Yā tiṇavatthārakassa kammassa kiriyā karaṇaṁ upagamanaṁ ajjhupagamanaṁ adhivāsanā appaṭikkosanā—
pli-tv-kd14:14.33.58.eng: the doing of, the performing of, the participation in, the consent to, the agreement to, the non-objection to that legal procedure of covering over as if with grass.

pli-tv-kd14:14.33.60.pli: Evaṁ vūpasantañce, bhikkhave, adhikaraṇaṁ paṭiggāhako ukkoṭeti, ukkoṭanakaṁ pācittiyaṁ;
pli-tv-kd14:14.33.60.eng: When a legal issue has been resolved like this, if a receiver of the confession reopens it, he incurs an offense entailing confession for the reopening.
pli-tv-kd14:14.33.60.comm: Again, “a receiver” should presumably be understood as any member of the Sangha who is taking part in the legal procedure.

pli-tv-kd14:14.33.61.eng: If anyone who gave their consent criticizes the resolution, they incur an offense entailing confession.

pli-tv-kd14:14.34.2.eng: There’s one way of settling a legal issue arising from business:

pli-tv-kd14:14.34.4.eng: The fourth chapter on the settling of legal issues is finished.
